evidence.quote_or_span: The prepared Nsp15 structure is based on the conformation of the protein complexed with tipiracil (PDB code 6WXC) prepared considering the pH of 6.2 and consists of His235 and His250 neutral at NE2 and ND1, respectively, and Lys290 positively charged

evolution_trajectory.state_before: N-NTD has 31 NMR conformations; a subset is needed for docking.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Using all 31 conformations is computationally expensive; a representative subset must capture flexibility.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Clustering the 31 conformations based on Arg102 and Tyr109 flexibility and selecting five representative structures will adequately represent N-NTD flexibility.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Cluster 31 NMR conformations of N-NTD (PDB 6YI3) with Glu174 in open conformation, select five representatives based on Arg102 and Tyr109 flexibility.
evolution_trajectory.observation: we provide to the users five distinct conformations of the N-NTD solved with NMR after clustering the 31 conformations containing the Glu174 on an opened conformation and selecting representative structures according to the flexibility of the residues Arg102 and Tyr109
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: A binding site for docking on N-NTD needs to be defined.

evidence.quote_or_span: we provide to the users five distinct conformations of the N-NTD solved with NMR after clustering the 31 conformations containing the Glu174 on an opened conformation and selecting representative structures according to the flexibility of the residues Arg102 and Tyr109

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Non-synonymous variations (NSVs) in SARS-CoV-2 targets may affect drug binding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which NSVs to include for each target?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Selecting NSVs based on impact on catalytic region, ligand interaction, amino acid properties, and literature evidence will capture relevant mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess parameters: residue occurrence in catalytic region, possible interaction with ligand, amino acid properties (hydropathy, charge, side chain). Select 19 NSVs across targets.

criteria: amino acid properties (hydropathy, charge, side chain)

evolution_trajectory.observation: We assessed some parameters for the mutation's choice, such as the impact of the residue's occurrence in the catalytic region and its possible interaction with a ligand and the amino acid properties (hydropathy, charge and side chain).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: With targets and mutations prepared, virtual screening can be performed.

evidence.quote_or_span: We assessed some parameters for the mutation's choice, such as the impact of the residue's occurrence in the catalytic region and its possible interaction with a ligand and the amino acid properties (hydropathy, charge and side chain).

We believe that the DockThor-VS web server is an excellent alternative for the development of drug repositioning research against COVID-19, not only for constructing drug libraries and therapeutic target structures available through the web server, but also for allowing studies involving new targets structures provided by the users and virtual screening experiments involving in-house ligand libraries.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Curated target structures and drug library ready.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which FDA-approved drugs show promising binding to SARS-CoV-2 targets?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Virtual screening of e-Drug3D against all six targets using wild type structures and ensemble docking will identify potential repurposing candidates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform virtual screening with DockThor-VS for e-Drug3D at reference pH (6.6 to 7.4) against PLpro, Mpro, RdRp, NendoU, Spike, N protein using wild type.
evolution_trajectory.observation: We performed virtual screening experiments with DockThor-VS for the e-Drug3D dataset at the reference pH (6.6 to 7.4) for all SARS-CoV-2 targets available at the platform so far (e.g., PLpro, Mpro, RdRp, NendoU, Spike and N protein) using the wild type genomic variant.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Results need to be analyzed, focusing on drugs under clinical trials.

evidence.quote_or_span: We performed virtual screening experiments with DockThor-VS for the e-Drug3D dataset at the reference pH (6.6 to 7.4) for all SARS-CoV-2 targets available at the platform so far (e.g., PLpro, Mpro, RdRp, NendoU, Spike and N protein) using the wild type genomic variant.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Virtual screening completed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which drugs under clinical trials show promising docking scores?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Docking scores will reveal drugs with high predicted affinity across multiple targets.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze docking scores of drugs under clinical trials against COVID-19, presented in Table 1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The virtual screening results of some drugs currently under clinical trials against COVID-19 are presented in Table 1.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Results must be interpreted with caution; docking scores are not for clinical guidance.

evidence.quote_or_span: The virtual screening results of some drugs currently under clinical trials against COVID-19 are presented in Table 1.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Docking scores available for drugs under clinical trials.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Some drugs may have mechanisms of action that allow inhibition of multiple SARS-CoV-2 targets.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some drugs may inhibit multiple SARS-CoV-2 targets, exhibiting multiple antiviral mechanisms.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose that drugs with known mechanisms of action may also inhibit one or more SARS-CoV-2 targets, leading to multiple antiviral effects.
evolution_trajectory.observation: However, it is also possible that some drugs with known mechanisms of action, related to the interaction with host proteins or even with the virus entry pathway, can also inhibit one or more targets of the pathogen, thus exhibiting multiple mechanisms for the antiviral effect.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: This hypothesis motivates further analysis of multi-target inhibition profiles.

evidence.quote_or_span: However, it is also possible that some drugs with known mechanisms of action, related to the interaction with host proteins or even with the virus entry pathway, can also inhibit one or more targets of the pathogen, thus exhibiting multiple mechanisms for the antiviral effect.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Docking scores available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Are the predicted affinities consistent with experimental data?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Most predicted binding affinities will be moderate, consistent with experimental studies showing modest inhibitory effects.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare predicted affinities with literature experimental data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: we found that the majority of the predicted binding affinities are moderate in the range of high to low micromolar affinity units. This result is expected since many drugs under clinical trials exhibited no activity or only modest inhibitory effects against SARS-CoV-2 in some experimental studies reported in the literature.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Despite moderate affinities, some drugs show promising multi-target profiles.

evidence.quote_or_span: we found that the majority of the predicted binding affinities are moderate in the range of high to low micromolar affinity units
This result is expected since many drugs under clinical trials exhibited no activity or only modest inhibitory effects against SARS-CoV-2 in some experimental studies reported in the literature

validation_methods: Comparison with experimental studies from literature
success_verification.final_conclusion: DockThor-VS provides a platform for virtual screening against SARS-CoV-2 targets, incorporating receptor flexibility and genetic variations. Ledipasvir, imatinib, lopinavir, and daclatasvir were identified as promising multi-target drugs. Multiple conformations reduce false negatives; mutations may confer resistance. Docking results are not for clinical guidance.
limitations: Docking results must not be used for clinical guidance; top hits need experimental validation.
Protein dynamic effects caused by mutations are not taken into account by the docking protocol.
Platform currently covers only six targets; continuous updates planned.

risk_notes: Added step 12 to cover K17 hypothesis on multi-target inhibition.
Corrected residue numbering in step 7 from Arg92 to Arg102.
Ensured step 10 observation includes pH range 6.6 to 7.4.
Reframed step 12 (now step 13) as comparative analysis decision.

source.title: Adaptive laboratory evolution accelerated glutarate production by Corynebacterium glutamicum

initial_request.research_problem: Low volumetric productivity of metabolically engineered C. glutamicum strain GluA producing glutarate via synthetic pathway.
initial_request.known_context: Strain GluA produces glutarate via a synthetic pathway but has low productivity.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Low glutarate productivity in strain GluA.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to improve glutarate production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Flux enforcement by gdh deletion couples growth to glutarate production, enabling selection of faster-growing mutants with improved production via ALE.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test hypothesis via ALE.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Serendipitous discovery of faster-growing variant prompts systematic ALE.

evidence.quote_or_span: The hypothesis that strains selected for faster glutarate-coupled growth by adaptive laboratory evolution show improved glutarate production was tested.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a starting strain with improved growth and production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: GabD^P134L mutation improves growth and glutarate production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use GluA T0 (GabD^P134L) as starting strain for ALE.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GluA T0 with GabD^P134L showed almost twofold higher glutarate production (45±2 mM) compared to native GabD (25±1 mM).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Start systematic ALE from GluA T0.

evidence.quote_or_span: Notably, in the presence of 10 and 20 mM glutarate $GabD^{P134L}$ activity was significantly higher than that of GabD (Fig.

Since growth was coupled to production by flux enforcement, an almost twofold higher glutarate production resulted using $GabD^{P134L}$ ( $45 \pm 2 \, mM$ ) instead of the native version of GabD ( $25 \pm 1 \, mM$ ).
As a consequence of introducing the amino acid exchange E686Q in GltB, strain GluA RG2 produced more glutarate (55±2 mM; +22%) and more of its direct precursor 5AVA (7±1 mM; +250%; Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: GluA T0 available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can ALE further improve growth and production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Serial transfer under flux enforcement will select faster-growing mutants with improved glutarate production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform ALE serial transfer from GluA T0 to T8.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ALE carried out in repeated batch cultivations; glycerol stocks made at each transfer.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze growth and production of evolved strains.

evidence.quote_or_span: At every transfer, one aliquot was streaked out on agar plates and three single colonies were selected and used to make glycerol stocks (triplicate glycerol stocks GluA T1 until triplicate glycerol stocks GluA T8).
ALE of C. glutamicum strain GluA T0 was carried out in repeated batch cultivations.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ALE completed up to T8.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the phenotypic improvements?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Evolved strains will show increased growth rate and glutarate production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize growth and production of evolved strains.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ALE strain GluA T7 accumulated 10% more glutarate and tenfold more 5AVA (20±1 mM compared to 2±0 mM) than the parent strain.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Identify genetic basis of improvements.

evidence.quote_or_span: ALE strain GluA T7 accumulated 10% more glutarate and tenfold more 5AVA (20±1 mM compared to 2±0 mM) than the parent strain

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Phenotypic improvements observed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the genetic changes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations in genome or plasmids are responsible.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform genome sequencing of GluA T5 and T7.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GluA T5: no mutations but increased plasmid copy numbers. GluA T7: two mutations: a deletion of 21 bp in tetA(Z) and SNP gltB^E686Q.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate causal mutations via reverse genetics.

evidence.quote_or_span: Surprisingly, genome sequencing of strain GluA T5 did neither reveal genome-based nor plasmid-based mutations in comparison to the non-evolved strain GluA T0.
analysis of the sequence reads mapped to DNA of the three expression plasmids showed increased plasmid copy numbers (PCN) of all three plasmids.
two mutations were identified in GluA T7 that were absent from strains GluA T0 and GluA T5.
a deletion of 21 bp in the coding sequence of the antibiotic resistance marker of this plasmid, i.e. in for tetracycline efflux permease gene tetA(Z)
4 Influence of the SNP $gltB^{E686Q}$ and the plasmid pEC-XT99A-tet $A(Z)^{\Delta 21bp}$ - $gabTD^{P134L}$ on maximal growth rate, and glutarate and 5AVA production by stepwise reverse genetics.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutations identified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutation is causal for improved production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: gltB^E686Q is the main causal mutation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct reverse genetics strains: GluA RG1 (mutated plasmid only), RG2 (gltB^E686Q only), RG3 (both).
evolution_trajectory.observation: RG2 improved growth and productivity to levels comparable to GluA T7; RG1 reduced performance; RG3 did not further improve. Glutarate 55±2 mM (+22%), 5AVA 7±1 mM (+250%).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test dependence on gdh deletion.

evidence.quote_or_span: As a consequence of introducing the amino acid exchange E686Q in GltB, strain GluA RG2 produced more glutarate (55±2 mM; +22%) and more of its direct precursor 5AVA (7±1 mM; +250%; Fig.

Since the glutarate titer was comparable for GluA RG2 and GluA RG3, a higher volumetric productivity for glutarate resulted when mutations $GltB^{E686Q}$ and the 21 bp deletion were both present (Table 1).
4 Influence of the SNP $gltB^{E686Q}$ and the plasmid pEC-XT99A-tet $A(Z)^{\Delta 21bp}$ - $gabTD^{P134L}$ on maximal growth rate, and glutarate and 5AVA production by stepwise reverse genetics.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: gltB^E686Q validated as causal.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the benefit depend on gdh deletion?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Beneficial effect of GltB^E686Q depends on gdh deletion.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test in lysine-producing strains with and without gdh deletion.
evolution_trajectory.observation: In gdh deletion background, gltB^E686Q restored lysine production; without gdh deletion, no significant effect. L-lysine decreased by about 38% upon gdh deletion.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Scale up production in bioreactors.

evidence.quote_or_span: As a consequence of the gdh deletion, production of L-lysine was decreased by about 38%

Upon introduction of the $\mathrm{GltB^{E686Q}}$ mutation into the $gdh$ deletion mutant GSLA2G, significantly more L-lysine was produced (compare strain GSLA2G:: $\mathrm{gltB^{E686Q}}$ with GSLA2G in Fig.
Introduction of the $\mathrm{GltB^{E686Q}}$ mutation into strain GSLA2 did not change L-lysine production significantly (compare strain $\mathrm{GSLA2::gltB^{E686Q}}$ with GSLA2 in Fig.
Therefore, the $GltB^{E686Q}$ mutation was introduced either into the plasmid-less precursor strain GSLA2, which overproduces L-lysine, or its derivative GSLA2G that carries the gdh deletion.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutation validated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does GluA T7 perform in controlled bioreactors?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: GluA T7 will outperform GluA T0 in batch bioreactor.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare GluA T0 and T7 in batch bioreactors.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GluA T7 clearly outcompeted its precursor strain regarding product titer, volumetric productivity, and yield.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test fed-batch for higher titer.

evidence.quote_or_span: GluA T7 clearly outcompeted its precursor strain regarding product titer, volumetric productivity, and yield
A baffled bioreactor with total a volume of 3.7 L was used (KLF, Bioengineering AG, Switzerland).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Batch results promising.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can fed-batch further improve titer and productivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Fed-batch will increase glutarate titer and productivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform fed-batch bioreactor with GluA T7.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Higher volumetric productivity for glutarate resulted when mutations GltB^E686Q and the 21 bp deletion were both present.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Optimize downstream processing.

evidence.quote_or_span: Since the glutarate titer was comparable for GluA RG2 and GluA RG3, a higher volumetric productivity for glutarate resulted when mutations $GltB^{E686Q}$ and the 21 bp deletion were both present (Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Production established.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to efficiently recover glutarate from broth?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Reactive extraction with T-C6 and 1-dodecanol in ethyl oleate can achieve high yields.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Optimize reactive extraction and re-extraction.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 58.1% glutarate yield in extraction; re-extraction with n-propylamine or n-butylamine up to 99% yield.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: The results clearly showed that with increasing T-C6 content, the amount of glutarate being extracted from the aqueous phases increased until reaching a plateau at 58.1% glutarate yield (for molar ratios of T-C6/glutarate=9/1 or higher).
The use of both WSA's as amine extractant led to maximum re-extraction yields of 99%.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Process demonstrated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the limitations and future directions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The mechanism of GltB^E686Q is not fully understood, and further improvements are possible.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitations and suggest future work.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mechanism of GltB^E686Q not fully understood; further improvements possible by combining with other strategies. One reason that full recovery of glutarate from fermentation broth could not be achieved in the initial reactive extraction step is the presence of strong electrolytes like chloride ions in the aqueous phase.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude study.

evidence.quote_or_span: One reason that a full recovery of glutarate from fermentation broth could not be achieved in the initial reactive extraction step is the presence of strong electrolytes like chloride ions in the aqueous phase

Since it is known that at high $\mathrm{NH}_3$ concentrations (up to $40~\mathrm{mM}$ ) GS/GOGAT compensates for the lack of GDH [25], it is conceivable that the GOGAT mutant $\mathrm{GltB}^{\mathrm{E686Q}}$ is active at higher nitrogen concentrations.

success_verification.final_conclusion: ALE with flux enforcement improved glutarate production; gltB^E686Q was causal and dependent on gdh deletion; GluA T7 achieved improved performance in fed-batch.
limitations: Mechanism of GltB^E686Q not fully understood
Full recovery not achieved in initial extraction due to electrolytes

risk_notes: Transcriptome analysis failure (K06) omitted as not required for good trajectory.
Fed-batch evidence reused from reverse genetics table; dedicated fed-batch evidence not available.

source.title: Scalable continuous evolution for the generation of diverse enzyme variants encompassing promiscuous activities

initial_request.research_problem: Recapitulating the depth and scale of natural ortholog evolution in the laboratory to generate diverse enzyme variants with expanded promiscuous activities.
initial_request.known_context: Classical directed evolution lacks the depth and scale of natural evolution. OrthoRep is a continuous evolution system that may overcome this limitation.
constraints: Selection only on primary activity (tryptophan synthesis from indole and serine)
Use of S. cerevisiae as host with TRP5 deletion
quantifiable_goals: Generate diverse TmTrpB variants with expanded promiscuous activities
Achieve robust growth without tryptophan in indole-supplemented media

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Classical directed evolution methods have limited depth and scale, failing to recapitulate natural ortholog diversity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can continuous evolution with OrthoRep generate diverse enzyme variants with expanded promiscuous activities when selecting only on primary activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: OrthoRep continuous evolution, by providing depth and scale, can generate enzyme variants with diverse promiscuous activities when selecting only on primary activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test the hypothesis by designing a selection system for TmTrpB in yeast.
evolution_trajectory.observation: OrthoRep may be considerably better poised for this challenge than classical directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design a selection system to evolve TmTrpB under pressure for primary activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: we recognized that our recently developed continuous evolution system, OrthoRep, may be considerably better poised for this challenge

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The selection system is designed but untested with existing TmTrpB variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can existing TmTrpB variants complement the TRP5 deletion?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: TmTriple and other variants may require high expression to complement.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test complementation of TRP5 deletion by TmTriple and other variants under different promoters.
evolution_trajectory.observation: TmTriple and other variants only complemented when expressed from a high-strength promoter, indicating substantial adaptation needed.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed with continuous evolution to improve TmTrpB activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: TmTriple, along with other TrpB variants tested, only supported complementation when expressed from a high-strength promoter

evolution_trajectory.state_before: TmTrpB variants require high expression to complement, indicating low activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can continuous evolution improve TmTrpB activity to support growth without tryptophan?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Continuous evolution with OrthoRep and error-prone TP-DNAP1 will generate variants with improved primary activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Evolve TmTrpB in multiple independent replicates using OrthoRep with error-prone TP-DNAP1, passaging under decreasing tryptophan.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Multiple independent evolution experiments were set up and passaged under decreasing tryptophan.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Monitor adaptation of populations to growth without tryptophan.

evidence.quote_or_span: All passages for evolution were carried out as 1:100 dilutions.
Resulting cultures were then passaged six additional times into growth medium lacking Trp.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Evolution experiments are ongoing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How many populations adapt to grow without tryptophan?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some populations will fully adapt after sufficient generations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess growth of evolved populations in indole-supplemented media lacking tryptophan.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Ten populations fully adapted to grow without Trp after 90–130 generations.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sequence the adapted populations to identify mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: All six of the 100 mL cultures, and four of the twenty 3 mL cultures fully adapted and were capable of robust growth in indole-supplemented media lacking Trp after 90–130 generations (13–20 passages) (Fig. 1b, Supplementary Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Ten populations are adapted but their genotypes are unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What mutations are present in the adapted populations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bulk sequencing will reveal consensus amino acid changes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: PCR-amplify and bulk-sequence TmTrpB alleles from adapted populations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Bulk sequencing revealed consensus amino acid changes per population.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze clonal diversity to uncover cryptic variation.

evidence.quote_or_span: For each of the 10 fully adapted populations, we PCR-amplified and bulk-sequenced the TmTrpB alleles on the p1 plasmid.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Consensus sequences show limited diversity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is there more diversity at the clonal level?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Clonal TmTrpBs contain more sequence diversity than consensus sequences, indicating cryptic genetic variation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence individual clones from adapted populations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Clonal TmTrpBs contained more sequence diversity than consensus sequences.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Clone variants for in vitro characterization.

evidence.quote_or_span: Unsurprisingly, this set of clonal TmTrpBs contained more sequence diversity than the consensus sequences of the ten populations from which they were taken.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Diverse clonal variants are identified but not characterized in vitro.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the in vitro properties of these variants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cloning variants into E. coli expression vectors will enable in vitro characterization.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Clone 60 randomly chosen TmTrpBs from ten populations into E. coli expression vectors.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 60 TmTrpBs were cloned into E. coli expression vectors for in vitro characterization.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sequence the cloned variants to assess diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: We cloned 60 randomly chosen TmTrpBs from the ten continuous evolution populations into E. coli expression vectors for in vitro characterization.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Cloned variants are available but their sequences are not fully analyzed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How diverse are the 60 cloned TmTrpBs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The variants will show extensive diversity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence the 60 cloned TmTrpBs and analyze mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The 60 TmTrpBs have extensive diversity with an average of 9.3 non-synonymous mutations per variant and 194 unique amino acid changes.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Compare interpopulation and intrapopulation diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: These 60 TmTrpBs represent extensive diversity, with an average of 9.3 (±2.8 s.d.) non-synonymous mutations per variant and a total of 194 unique amino acid changes across the set

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Diverse variants are available but their promiscuous activities are unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do any variants exhibit unique promiscuous activities?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some variants may accept non-native substrates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test 60 variants and benchmark TrpBs for activity with 5-CF₃ substrate.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variant Tri-100-1-G uniquely accepted 5-CF₃ as a substrate, while all other variants and benchmark TrpBs showed no detectable product formation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze the sources of diversity and promiscuity.

evidence.quote_or_span: the ability to accept 5-CF₃ as a substrate was unique to Tri-100-1-G: all other variants, as well as the benchmark TrpBs, showed no detectable product formation with this substrate

Preparations of TmTrpBs WT-003-1-A, Q90\*-003-1-A, and Tri-100-2-A, the 60 new variants, and four top-performing TrpB benchmark variants from past classical directed evolution campaigns (including TmTriple) were all tested for product formation with indole by UV absorption and nine indole analogs by high performance liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (HPLC-MS) to detect substrate promiscuity (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Diverse variants with unique promiscuous activities are observed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What factors contributed to the observed diversity and promiscuity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Evolutionary depth, scale, and cryptic genetic variation from non-neutral drift on multicopy plasmids contribute to promiscuity diversity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Attribute diversity to depth, scale, and cryptic variation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The authors attribute diversity to evolutionary depth (many rounds) and scale (independent replicates), and propose that cryptic genetic variation from non-neutral drift on multicopy plasmids contributes to promiscuity diversity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations compared to natural orthologs.

evidence.quote_or_span: A partial explanation may come from the high primary activities of OrthoRep-evolved TmTrpBs, as validated by kinetic measurements showing that variants tested have $k_{cat}/K_{M}$ values for indole well in the $10^{5} M^{-1} s^{-1}$ range.

Since OrthoRep drove the evolution of TmTrpB in a continuous format for >100 generations, each resulting TmTrpB is the outcome of many rounds of evolutionary improvement and change (evolutionary depth).
We attribute this to the fact that we ran our evolution experiments in multiple independent replicates (evolutionary scale).

A third explanation for the promiscuous profile diversity of these TmTrpB variants is that each replicate evolution experiment had, embedded within it, mechanisms to generate cryptic genetic variation without strong selection on primary activity.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The evolved diversity is characterized but not compared to natural orthologs.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Evolved diversity is lower than natural ortholog diversity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare sequence divergence of evolved variants to natural orthologs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Evolved TmTrpBs have lower diversity than natural orthologs (median divergence 4.3%, maximum 8%).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Propose future scaling and generalization.

evidence.quote_or_span: the median divergence between pairs of variants from our experiment is 4.3% with a maximum of 8%

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The approach shows promise but has lower diversity than nature.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can this strategy be generalized to other enzymes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Continuous replicate evolution selecting only on primary activity will become a general strategy for expanding promiscuous activities.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose generalization of the approach.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The authors hypothesize that continuous replicate evolution selecting only on primary activity will become a general strategy for expanding promiscuous activities.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude the study.

evidence.quote_or_span: we envision that the process of continuous replicate evolution, selecting only on primary activities of enzymes, will become a general strategy for expanding promiscuous activity ranges of enzymes as we and others extend it to new targets.

validation_methods: Growth assays in indole-supplemented media lacking tryptophan
metrics: Number of consensus and clonal mutations
success_verification.final_conclusion: OrthoRep continuous evolution successfully generated diverse TmTrpB variants with expanded promiscuous activities, including a variant uniquely accepting 5-CF₃, by selecting only on primary activity. The diversity is attributed to evolutionary depth, scale, and cryptic genetic variation.
limitations: Evolved diversity is lower than natural orthologs (median 4.3% vs. 11% for human-mouse orthologs)
Only one unique promiscuous activity (5-CF₃) was identified among 60 variants
The approach has only been demonstrated on TmTrpB

source.title: COMPASS for rapid combinatorial optimization of biochemical pathways based on artificial transcription factors

initial_request.research_problem: Achieving balanced expression of multiple genes in synthetic pathways in S. cerevisiae, where optimal expression levels are unknown a priori, and existing combinatorial assembly methods are limited by sequence dependency and E. coli-based assembly difficulties.
initial_request.known_context: Balanced expression of multiple genes is central for establishing new biosynthetic pathways or multiprotein cellular complexes. High-level expression of pathway genes often increases metabolic burden. Golden Gate cloning is limited by sequence dependency and E. coli assembly difficulties.
constraints: Optimal expression levels unknown a priori
Sequence dependency of restriction enzyme-based assembly
Difficulty of assembling large plasmids in E. coli
quantifiable_goals: Rapid combinatorial assembly of up to ten pathway genes
Decouple growth and production phases to reduce metabolic burden

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Balanced expression of multiple genes is difficult due to unknown optimal levels, and high-level expression often increases metabolic burden. Existing Golden Gate cloning methods are limited by sequence dependency and E. coli assembly difficulties.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to achieve balanced expression of multiple pathway genes while minimizing metabolic burden and overcoming limitations of current assembly methods.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Inducible artificial transcription factors (ATFs) can allow conditional expression of genes, decoupling growth and production phases and reducing metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop a method using inducible ATFs to control pathway gene expression.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Inducible ATFs allow a conditional expression of genes.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design a cloning system based on this hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Balanced expression of multiple genes is central for establishing new biosynthetic pathways or multiprotein cellular complexes.
high-level expression of pathway genes often increases metabolic burden
The most significant limitation of RE-based assembly methods is their sequence dependency, as RE IIs recognition sites must be eliminated from all DNA sequences to be assembled (“DNA domestication”).

GG-based methods are entirely based on construction steps performed in E. coli, which often is not straightforward, in particular for assemblies of genes of long pathways; amplification of large plasmids is not easily done in E. coli

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that inducible ATFs can decouple growth and production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a cloning system that enables rapid combinatorial assembly of pathways with ATF control.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A three-level cloning system with positive selection and CRISPR/Cas9 integration can efficiently generate combinatorial libraries.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design COMPASS with three-level cloning (Entry, Acceptor, Destination vectors) and positive selection.
evolution_trajectory.observation: COMPASS requires only 11 core vectors to generate libraries of millions of constructs for ten-gene pathways.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test ATF activity in genomic context to validate the design.

evidence.quote_or_span: COMPASS allows the rapid combinatorial assembly of up to ten pathway genes, each transcriptionally controlled by nine inducible ATF/binding site (BS) units
COMPASS makes use of positive selection protocols, which severely reduces the need for checking individual constructs
COMPASS is equipped with multi-locus CRISPR/Cas9-mediated modification capacity

An important aspect of COMPASS is that it only requires 11 core vectors (one Entry, eight Acceptor, and two Destination vectors) to establish combinatorial libraries of thousands to millions of constructs for ten-gene pathways.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: COMPASS design established; need to validate ATF activity in genomic context.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether genomic position affects ATF transcriptional output.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Genomic position may affect ATF activity but relative strengths should be preserved.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Integrate nine ATF/BS regulators into a second locus (XII-5) and quantify transcriptional output.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Genomic position affected transcriptional output, but relative strengths of regulators were preserved.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to combinatorial assembly of pathway modules.

evidence.quote_or_span: we tested whether the genomic locus into which the nine regulators are integrated affects their transcriptional activity. To this end, we placed them into a second locus, XII-5, and quantified the transcriptional output and noise level.

the genomic position affected the transcriptional output of the plant regulators. However, the expressional effect exerted by the different regulators was well preserved. The strongest regulators always led to the highest expression mean of the reporter protein, regardless of where the regulator was placed in the genome

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ATF activity validated; ready to assemble pathway modules.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether the three-level cloning with positive selection works for pathway assembly.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Combinatorial cloning of ATF/BS-CDS modules into Destination vector I using four rounds of assembly with positive selection will yield correct constructs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform four rounds of assembly with positive selection on selective media.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Yeast cells with successful constructs grew on selective media, confirming assembly.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Measure growth defect upon induction to assess metabolic burden.

evidence.quote_or_span: Yeast cells with successful constructs grow on SC-Ura/-His medium.
Yeast cells with successful constructs grow on SC-Ura/-His/-Leu medium.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pathway assembled; need to quantify metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether induction of pathway genes causes growth defect.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Induction will cause a measurable growth defect due to metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure growth rate of strain MXintpDI 1 in inducing vs non-inducing medium.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strain MXintpDI 1 showed a 5.5% reduction in growth rate in inducing medium compared to WT.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen combinatorial libraries for production phenotypes.

evidence.quote_or_span: Compared to WT, strain MXintpDI 1 did not show a growth defect in noninducing medium, while it showed a 5.5% reduction in growth rate in inducing medium

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Growth defect quantified; ready to screen libraries for production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What fraction of library members produce the target compound?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A significant fraction may be non-productive due to suboptimal expression levels.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-engineer β-ionone and naringenin pathways and screen 20 colonies by flow cytometry.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Approximately 30% of library members showed no or low naringenin production.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze genotypes of better producers to identify patterns.

evidence.quote_or_span: Flow cytometry revealed that approximately 30% of the library members show no- or low-NG production (Narion 1–6; Fig. 6c, Supplementary Data 13).
The better producers (Narion 16–20) harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes (except Narion 17 and 20, which both contain strong ATF/BS units upstream of AtPAL-2).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Screening revealed ~30% non-productive members.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What ATF/BS combinations correlate with better production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Better producers harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of naringenin genes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence ATF/BS units in top producers (Narion 16–20).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Better producers harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes, with exceptions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitations of current COMPASS version.

evidence.quote_or_span: The better producers (Narion 16–20) harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes (except Narion 17 and 20, which both contain strong ATF/BS units upstream of AtPAL-2).
Flow cytometry revealed that approximately 30% of the library members show no- or low-NG production (Narion 1–6; Fig. 6c, Supplementary Data 13).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: COMPASS successfully demonstrated for up to 10 genes.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Pathways with more than 10 genes cannot be assembled with current vectors.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Additional plasmids can extend capacity beyond 10 genes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Include plasmids pCOM001 and pCOM009 in the toolkit to extend capacity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Current COMPASS version limited to 10 genes; additional plasmids extend capacity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Summarize design advantage of decoupling growth and production.

evidence.quote_or_span: To extend the capacity of COMPASS even further—for pathways with more than ten genes—we included two additional plasmids (pCOM001 and pCOM009) in the toolkit.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Limitation addressed; overall design validated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Confirm that COMPASS decouples growth and production phases.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: COMPASS employs modular and inducible transcription factor-based controllers that allow decoupling growth and production phases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate by measuring growth rates after induction.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Growth rates decrease by 7–16% after induction, confirming decoupling.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude that COMPASS is effective for combinatorial pathway optimization.

evidence.quote_or_span: COMPASS employs modular and inducible transcription factor-based controllers that allow decoupling growth and production phases.

metrics: ATF/BS unit strength correlation with production
success_verification.final_conclusion: COMPASS enables rapid combinatorial optimization of pathways by decoupling growth and production using inducible ATFs, with a capacity of up to 10 genes extendable by additional plasmids.
limitations: Current version limited to 10 genes without extension plasmids
~30% of library members are non-productive
Growth defect of 5.5% observed in one strain

source.title: GREACE-assisted adaptive laboratory evolution in endpoint fermentation broth enhances lysine production by Escherichia coli

initial_request.research_problem: Late-stage fermentation broth (EFB) contains high concentrations of lysine and leaked cellular metabolites, causing multifactorial stress that limits lysine production in E. coli. Existing ALE methods use single-factor stress, which may not reflect real industrial conditions and can lead to evolutionary trade-offs.
initial_request.known_context: EFB exerts multifactorial stress; single-stress ALE can cause trade-offs (e.g., ethanol tolerance vs. acid resistance).
constraints: Must avoid trade-offs seen in single-stress ALE
Must stabilize genotype after evolution
quantifiable_goals: Improve lysine titer, yield, and total production
Enhance stress tolerance in EFB

evolution_trajectory.state_before: EFB contains high lysine and leaked metabolites, causing multifactorial stress that limits production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Single-stress ALE may not confer tolerance to real industrial conditions and can cause trade-offs.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ALE in real EFB with controlled mutagenesis will improve stress tolerance and lysine production without trade-offs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use GREACE-assisted ALE in EFB with timed mutagenesis.

tool_or_method.name: GREACE (Genome Replication Engineering Assisted Continuous Evolution)

evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated based on known limitations of single-stress ALE.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design a modified GREACE system for timed mutagenesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Late-stage fermentation broth contains high concentrations of target chemicals. Additionally, it contains various cellular metabolites which have leaked from lysed cells, which would exert multifactorial stress to industrial hyperproducers and perturb both cellular metabolism and product formation.
In order to enhance bacterial stress tolerance to actual industrial production conditions, ALE in late-stage fermentation broth is desired.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Need for controlled mutagenesis during ALE in EFB.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Standard GREACE uses constitutive mutagenesis; need timed control to avoid instability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Expressing DnaQ mutant KR5-2 under arabinose-inducible promoter on temperature-sensitive plasmid will allow timed mutagenesis.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Modify GREACE by placing KR5-2 under P_BAD promoter on temperature-sensitive plasmid pKAR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Design yields a system where mutagenesis is induced only when arabinose is added and plasmid is maintained at permissive temperature.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Implement GREACE-assisted ALE in EFB with stepwise lysine increase.

evidence.quote_or_span: GREACE was first modified by expressing a DnaQ mutant KR5-2 using an arabinose inducible promoter on a temperature-sensitive plasmid, which resulted in timed mutagenesis introduction.

The whole process was performed within only 7 transfers

source.title: Representability of algebraic topology for biomolecules in machine learning based scoring and virtual screening

initial_request.research_problem: Conventional persistent homology loses chemical/biological information during topological simplification, limiting representational power for biomolecules.
initial_request.known_context: Persistent homology has been used for biomolecular analysis but treats atoms as homogeneous points, discarding element identities and non-covalent interactions.
constraints: Must retain chemical/biological information during topological simplification
Must be applicable to large-scale virtual screening
quantifiable_goals: Improve binding affinity prediction (Pearson correlation, RMSE)
Improve virtual screening AUC over AutoDock Vina

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Persistent homology applied to biomolecules treats all atoms as identical points, losing element-specific chemical information.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to incorporate chemical and biological information into topological representations of biomolecules.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multi-component persistent homology, by selecting subsets of atoms based on element types, can retain critical chemical/biological information during topological simplification.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose multi-component persistent homology to address information loss.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Conventional persistent homology neglects chemical and biological information; multi-component approach can retain it by treating different atom types separately.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Extend the idea to capture non-covalent interactions via multi-level persistent homology.

evidence.quote_or_span: Conventional persistent homology neglects chemical and biological information during the topological abstraction and thus has limited representational power for complex chemical and biological systems.
multi-component persistent homology retains critical chemical and biological information during the topological simplification of biomolecular geometric complexity.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Multi-level persistent homology captures interaction types but does not explicitly include partial charge information.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to incorporate electrostatic interactions into topological invariants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Electrostatic persistence, by using a distance function based on partial charges, can embed electrostatic information into topological invariants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Introduce electrostatic persistence to incorporate partial charge information.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Electrostatic persistence incorporates partial charge information into topological invariants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Pair topological methods with Wasserstein distance and integrate with ML for predictive tasks.

evidence.quote_or_span: Electrostatic persistence incorporates partial charge information into topological invariants.
We introduce electrostatics interaction induced distance functions in Eq (10) to address the electrostatic interactions among charged atoms.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Topological descriptors (multi-component, multi-level, electrostatic) are defined but need to be compared and used for prediction.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to measure similarity between topological representations and integrate with machine learning.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Wasserstein distance can measure similarity between barcodes, and integration with KNN, ensemble trees, and deep CNNs will enable binding analysis and virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Pair topological methods with Wasserstein distance and integrate with KNN, ensemble trees, and deep CNNs.

evidence.quote_or_span: These topological methods are paired with Wasserstein distance to characterize similarities between molecules and are further integrated with a variety of machine learning algorithms, including k-nearest neighbors, ensemble of trees, and deep convolutional neural networks, to manifest their descriptive and predictive powers for protein-ligand binding analysis and virtual screening of small molecules.
Table 8. Performance of different approaches on the S1322 dataset.

evidence.quote_or_span: This observation partially answers the question that in the cases where the small molecules are relatively simple and are relatively of similar size, multi-level persistent homology is able to enrich the characterization of the small molecules which further improves the robustness of the model.

It is interesting to note that although Wasserstein metric based KNN methods are not as accurate as GBT approaches, the consensus result obtained by averaging over various predictions with Wasserstein metric on different sets of barcodes is quite accurate.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Ligand-based experiments show GBT effectiveness; complex-based prediction requires modeling protein-ligand interactions.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Evaluate TopBP-ML(Complex) and TopBP-DL(Complex) on PDBBind v2007, v2013, v2015, v2016.

evolution_trajectory.observation: TopBP(Complex) consensus model (average of ML and DL) achieves best performance on all test sets.

evidence.quote_or_span: we investigate the representational power of the proposed topological method on a few benchmark protein-ligand binding affinity data sets, namely, PDBBind v2007, PDBBind v2013, PDBBind v2015 and PDBBind v2016 [99].

In TopBP-ML(Complex), alpha complex is used to describe the arrangement of carbon and heavy atom networks, while Rips complex with different distance matrices is used to describe the protein-ligand interactions from the perspective of interaction distances and strength of electrostatics interactions.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use optimal features and models to guide virtual screening design.

evidence.quote_or_span: TopBP(Complex) delivers the best prediction performance on all four testing sets.
The inferior performance of convolutional neural networks in v2007 might be a result of the small data size.
topological deep convolutional neural networks are able to outperform the topological GBT algorithm in predicting v2013, v2015 and v2016 core sets.

evidence.quote_or_span: The optimal selection of features and methods are determined by studying the first two applications and this finding leads to the main application studied in this work, the topological structure-based virtual screening.

The dataset is much larger than the two applications on protein-ligand binding affinity prediction which makes parameter tuning very time consuming. Therefore, the best performing procedures in ligand-based binding affinity prediction and protein-ligand-complex-based binding affinity prediction are applied in this virtual screening application.

evidence.quote_or_span: Same as the idea of taking the average output of different ensemble of trees models as the final output in TopVS-ML, we add TopVS-DL as another voter to TopVS-ML to construct a final model, called TopVS.
When only topological descriptor of small molecules are used, which falls into the category of ligand-based virtual screening, an AUC of 0.81 is achieved.

An AUC of 0.83 is obtained with a model combining both sets of descriptors which is better than each individual performance, suggesting that the two groups of descriptors are complementary to each other
For the topological learning model using only the descriptions of protein-ligand interactions, an AUC of 0.77 is achieved.

evidence.quote_or_span: We have also generated virtual screening results of AutoDock Vina (ADV) based on the computed binding free energy by ADV and compared them with those of the present TopVS in terms of enrichment factors and the areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC).
Our model with small molecular descriptors delivers an AUC of 0.81, which is comparably well to the other top performing methods.

evolution_trajectory.observation: For good docking targets, complex features AUC 0.86 vs ligand 0.81; for poor docking, complex features AUC 0.74 vs ligand 0.82. Marginal overall improvement due to varying docking quality.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude with limitations and future directions.

evidence.quote_or_span: For the targets with high quality results by Autodock Vina (AUC of ADV > 0.8), the ligand-based features achieve an AUC of 0.81 and the complex-based features achieve an AUC of 0.86.
for the targets with low quality results by Autodock Vina (AUC of ADV < 0.5), the ligand-based features achieve an AUC of 0.82 and the complex-based features achieve an AUC of 0.74.
The marginal improvement made by protein-compound complexes may be due to the various docking quality.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Complex-based features have been evaluated, but their robustness to element combinations and the role of electrostatics need further analysis.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: GBT is robust to redundant element combinations; electrostatic embedding improves performance; rare elements require both training samples and features.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze feature robustness by incrementally adding element combinations and examining electrostatic and rare element effects.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GBT models are robust against inclusion of more element combinations; electrostatic interactions play an important role; rare elements require both training samples and features.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude with overall findings and limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: To test whether this redundancy causes degenerated results of the model, the features of one element combination is added to the model at a step and the model is validated with an accumulation of the added features at each step.
For most cases in Fig 4, the model is robust against the inclusion of more element combinations.
the performance fluctuates within a small range, which indicates that the present method is reasonably stable against the increase in element combinations
GBT algorithms are not very sensitive to redundant features and are robust against overfitting

validation_methods: Comparison with AutoDock Vina on DUD
success_verification.final_conclusion: Topological learning methods (TopBP, TopVS) outperform existing methods in binding affinity prediction and virtual screening; ligand-based features are robust, while complex-based features depend on docking quality.
limitations: Complex-based feature performance highly depends on docking quality
CNN underperforms on small datasets (PDBBind v2007)
Rare elements require both training samples and features

source.title: A machine learning approach for reliable prediction of amino acid interactions and its application in the directed evolution of enantioselective enzymes

initial_request.research_problem: Directed evolution of enantioselective enzymes is limited by epistasis, where non-additive effects of mutations impair prediction of improved variants. The goal is to develop a machine learning method that predicts amino acid interactions from sequence and fitness data alone, without requiring 3D structure, to guide the engineering of enantioselective enzymes.
initial_request.known_context: Epistasis impairs predictions in protein engineering; machine learning has not been applied to evolve enzyme mutants with enhanced or inverted stereoselectivity.
constraints: No 3D structure required
Only sequence and fitness data
input_data: 9 single point mutants of ANEH with experimental E-values
quantifiable_goals: Predict improved enantioselective variants beyond LW202 (E=115)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Epistasis impairs predictions in protein engineering; machine learning not applied to stereoselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to capture non-additive mutational effects without 3D structure?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Digital signal processing (FFT) and protein spectra can model global sequence effects to predict fitness and identify improved mutants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose innov'SAR approach using FFT on amino acid sequences.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FFT-based protein spectrum may capture epistasis by considering whole-sequence impact.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design the innov'SAR methodology with encoding, modelling, and predictive phases.

evidence.quote_or_span: Epistasis phenomena can impair the predictions in protein engineering and screening.
In this sense, machine learning has not been applied in order to evolve enzyme mutants with enhanced or inverted stereoselectivity.

We propose a new approach based only on the amino acids sequence and using digital signal processing (FFT) and protein spectrum to modelling and predict the biological activity/fitness and for identifying combination of single points amino acid substitutions with improved fitness.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that FFT can capture epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a concrete methodology to encode sequences, build models, and validate predictions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: innov'SAR with three phases (encoding via AAindex + FFT, modelling via PLS regression, predictive phase) can predict fitness.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design innov'SAR: encode sequences using AAindex, apply FFT to get protein spectrum, use PLS regression, validate with LOOCV and R²/RMSE.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Methodology defined; multiple AAindex indices to be evaluated for best model.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Collect initial experimental data (9 single mutants) to build first model.

evidence.quote_or_span: innov'SAR consists of 3 phases, namely, the encoding phase, the modelling phase and the predictive phase.
a Fast Fourier transform is applied to convert the encoded sequence into a protein spectrum
innov'SAR used a partial least square regression, PLS, as algorithm of regression
The method of cross-validation used for this study is the Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV)
n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Use 9 experimentally assessed single point mutants of ANEH as dataset A.

evidence.quote_or_span: n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.
As noted above, in the original 2006-study, nine single point mutations of ANEH were experimentally evolved and assessed for their impact on the enantioselectivity $^{49}$ .
After 5 iterative rounds of saturation mutagenesis, a total of nine single point mutations led to an improved variant (LW202) with an E-value of 115.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Nine single mutants available; 28 recombinants previously reported.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do recombinants exhibit epistasis?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple mutants will show non-additive effects (epistasis).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze 28 multiple point mutants for epistasis by comparing experimental vs. additive predictions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All 28 recombinants show epistasis (9 positive, 19 negative).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Build model DSA_FFT to capture epistasis.

evidence.quote_or_span: It turned out that all of the 28 variants with multiple mutations have a positive or a negative epistasis: 9 with positive epistasis and 19 with negative epistasis
It turned out that all of the 28 variants with multiple mutations have a positive or a negative epistasis: 9 with positive epistasis and 19 with negative epistasis (Table S4).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Epistasis confirmed; need model to capture non-additive effects.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can innov'SAR with FFT predict epistatic interactions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Model DSA_FFT trained on 9 single mutants will outperform additive model for recombinants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build DSA_FFT using AAindex ZHOH040101 and FFT; evaluate with LOOCV on 28 recombinants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: DSA_FFT gives better predictions than additive model for 18 of 28 mutants, capturing epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Improve model by including multiple mutants in training set.

evidence.quote_or_span: The innov'SAR model allows a distinct capture of epistasis for these mutants
Among the 28 multiple point mutants, the model DSA\_FFT gives better results for 18 mutants than the prediction based only on the addition of mutations (Fig.

After the evaluation of multiple encoding indexes by innov'SAR, described previously, the encoding index ZHOH040101 $^{54}$ was identified as one of the best to generate an accurate model by innov'SAR and selected to build the model DSA\_FFT.
n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSA_FFT captures some epistasis but limited by training on singles only.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Adding 28 recombinants to training set (total 37 mutants) will improve accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build DSB_FFT on 37 mutants (9 singles + 28 multiples); evaluate with LOOCV.
evolution_trajectory.observation: DSB_FFT achieves LOOCV R²=0.96, better than DSA_FFT; predicts 22 of 28 recombinants better than additive.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test necessity of FFT by ablation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the addition of multiple point mutants to the learning dataset includes some information about the epistasis between mutations and thus improves the accuracy
the higher $R^{2}$ value (0.96) for the LOOCV training set
Among the 28 multiple point mutants, the model DSB\_FFT generates more accurate predictions for 22 mutants, better results than the one based only on the addition of mutations (see Fig.

We created a validation dataset with the 28 multiple point mutants (Table S3), including the mutant LW202, that were previously reported (among a total of 512) $^{50}$ , in order to check the accuracy of our models based on only 9 single point mutations.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.
With only 37 mutants, our model was able to predict the potential outcome of 512, which means that on the basis of only 7% of the mutants, a whole fitness landscape was predicted.

evolution_trajectory.observation: DSB_noFFT achieves R²=0.88, lower than DSB_FFT (0.96); FFT improves epistasis capture.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that only FFT-based model can find mutants better than LW202.

evidence.quote_or_span: This model has 0.88 as value of $R^{2}$
the higher $R^{2}$ value (0.96) for the LOOCV training set

In this case the innov'SAR application comprising FFT was the only method that could predict any improved recombinants, while a pure additive model and innov'SAR without FFT proposed at best only the already known experimental LW202 mutant.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSB_noFFT less accurate; need to confirm FFT's unique ability to identify improved mutants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can additive or noFFT models predict mutants better than LW202?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Only FFT-based model can predict variants exceeding LW202 (E=115).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare predictions from additive model, DSB_noFFT, and DSB_FFT for all 512 variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Additive model (R²=0.62) and noFFT (R²=0.64) fail to predict any mutant better than LW202; only DSB_FFT succeeds.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use DSB_FFT to predict and rank all 512 possible variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: FFT is an improving factor for the predictions of mutants with epistasis in the models generated by innov'SAR.
the predictions based on only the addition of mutations cannot find any better mutants than the mutant LW202
The prediction based solely on descriptors without FFT also failed to give predictions of enantioselectivity better than mutant LW202
We found 0.62 and 0.64 as $R^{2}$ values, respectively, for the predictions based on the addition of mutations and for the predictions generated without FFT.
The incorporation of the 28-multiple mutation variants, including the mutant LW202, into the dataset A should improve the accuracy of the model for the prediction of the 512 possible variants.

We could identify and experimentally confirmed extremely fit mutants out of 512 possible mutational combinations by using a dataset made of only $7\%$ of the mutants contained in the total fitness landscape.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: DSB_FFT can rank all 512 possible variants by predicted E-value.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Computationally generate all 2^9=512 variants and predict E-values using DSB_FFT.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 474 new variants predicted; top candidates identified for experimental validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: we generated computationally all combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ variants)
This approach resulted in $2^{9}-38=474$ new variants with multiple point mutations.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.

evidence.quote_or_span: We were able to demonstrate that the 5 mutants have properties that corresponded quite well to the predictions.

a $R^{2}$ value of 0.94 was obtained
These are distinctly better than mutant LW202 (E = 115) evolved in our original study.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.
