RMCE limited by availability of pseudo sites and cognate enzymes.

source.title: Virological characteristics of the SARS-CoV-2 XBB variant derived from recombination of two Omicron subvariants

initial_request.research_problem: Investigate the virological characteristics of the SARS-CoV-2 XBB variant, a recombinant lineage from two Omicron subvariants (BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1), to understand how recombination impacts viral fitness, immune evasion, receptor binding, fusogenicity, and pathogenicity, and to explain XBB's rapid global spread.
initial_request.known_context: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1, with a breakpoint in the RBD. It shows higher effective reproduction number (Re) than parental lineages, indicating increased fitness through recombination.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The study aims to investigate the virological characteristics of the XBB variant, which emerged through recombination of two Omicron subvariants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The origin and fitness advantage of XBB are unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 and has higher fitness than parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform phylogenetic and recombination analyses to determine origin and estimate Re.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 with breakpoint at position 22,920; tMRCA July 7, 2022.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Establishing recombination origin motivates investigation of fitness advantage.

evidence.quote_or_span: Our phylogenetic analyses suggested that XBB emerged through the recombination of two cocirculating BA.2 lineages, BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 (a progeny of BA.2.75), during the summer of 2022.

Our analysis suggests that the XBB clade's most recent common ancestor (tMRCA) existed at the start of July 2022 (median posterior date: July 7, 2022; 95% HPD confidence intervals: from June 10, 2022, to July 29, 2022).
we first split the alignment used for the recombination analysis above at genome position 22,920 (the breakpoint inferred by RDP5)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether recombination directly increases fitness is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB shows higher effective reproduction number (Re) than parental lineages, indicating increased fitness through recombination.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Estimate Re for XBB and parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB shows substantially higher Re than parental lineages, suggesting recombination increased viral fitness.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Higher Re validates the novel finding of fitness increase via recombination.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB shows substantially higher $R_{e}$ than the parental lineages, suggesting that the recombination event increased $R_{e}$ (i.e., viral fitness).
Our phylogenetic analyses suggested that XBB emerged through the recombination of two co-circulating BA.2 lineages, BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 (a progeny of BA.2.75), during the summer of 2022

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB shows higher Re than parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether recombination directly increases fitness is novel.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB is the first SARS-CoV-2 variant to increase fitness through recombination.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate the novelty of recombination-driven fitness increase.
evolution_trajectory.observation: This is the first documented example of a SARS-CoV-2 variant increasing its fitness through recombination rather than substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Novel fitness mechanism prompts investigation of immune resistance.

evidence.quote_or_span: To our knowledge, this is the first documented example of a SARS-CoV-2 variant increasing its fitness through recombination rather than substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 is a subvariant of XBB.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Immune resistance of XBB.1 to breakthrough infection sera is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 exhibits profound resistance to BA.2 and BA.5 breakthrough infection sera.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform neutralization assays using pseudoviruses and sera from BA.2 and BA.5 breakthrough infections.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 shows 30-fold resistance to BA.2 sera (P=0.0002) and 13-fold resistance to BA.5 sera (P<0.0001). 10/14 BA.2 sera and 9/20 BA.5 sera fail to neutralize.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Profound resistance leads to dissection of individual substitution contributions.

evidence.quote_or_span: we found that XBB.1 exhibits profound (30-fold) resistance to breakthrough BA.2 infection sera (P=0.0002, Fig. 2a).
XBB.1 exhibited profound (13-fold) resistance to breakthrough BA.5 infection sera (P<0.0001, Fig. 2b).
10 out of 14 breakthrough BA.2 infection sera and 9 out of 20 breakthrough BA.5 infection sera fail to neutralize XBB.1.
XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.
5 sequences harboring any of the convergent S substitutions, S:R346X, S:K444X, and S:N460X from our dataset in order to exclude the sequences belonging to the recent BA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 shows profound immune resistance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which spike substitutions contribute to immune resistance?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple single substitutions in XBB.1 S protein confer significant resistance to BA.2 and BA.5 sera, with cooperative effects.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test individual and combined substitutions using mutant pseudoviruses.
evolution_trajectory.observation: V83A (2.1-fold), Y144del (2.9-fold), F486S (3.0-fold), F490S (2.7-fold) confer resistance to BA.2 sera. Y144del and G446S cooperatively contribute to BA.5 resistance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Understanding substitution effects informs vaccine escape assessment.

evidence.quote_or_span: several substitutions, such as V83A (2.1-fold, P=0.0034), Y144del (2.9-fold, P=0.0002), Q183E (2.0-fold, P=0.0039), R346T (2.1-fold, P=0.0005), L368I (1.8-fold, P=0.042), V445P (2.1-fold, P=0.0002), F486S (3.0-fold, P=0.0002), and F490S (2.7-fold, P=0.024), conferred significant resistance to breakthrough BA.2 infection sera.
our data suggest that multiple substitutions in the XBB.1S cooperatively contribute to the resistance against humoral immunity induced by breakthrough BA.2 infection.
these two mutations (Y144del and G446S) cooperatively contribute to the resistance against humoral immunity induced by breakthrough BA.5 infection.
XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.
SARS-CoV-2 spike L452R variant evades cellular immunity and increases infectivity.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 evades infection-induced immunity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does XBB.1 escape vaccine-induced immunity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 significantly escapes from 4-dose vaccine sera, including monovalent, BA.1 bivalent, and BA.5 bivalent vaccines.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test neutralization by sera from individuals receiving 4 doses of various vaccines.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 escapes monovalent (23.3-fold), BA.1 bivalent (19-fold), and BA.5 bivalent (41-fold) vaccine sera (P<0.0001).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Vaccine escape data contributes to antigenic distinctness analysis.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 evades humoral immunity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does XBB.1 RBD bind ACE2 compared to BA.2?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 RBD has higher ACE2 binding affinity than BA.2, with F486S reducing affinity but compensated by other substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure ACE2 binding affinity of XBB.1 RBD and mutants using surface plasmon resonance.
evolution_trajectory.observation: R346T and L368I increase ACE2 binding affinity; F486S decreases affinity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: ACE2 binding affinity data leads to infectivity assessment.

evidence.quote_or_span: the R346T substitution, which is common in both XBB.1 and BQ.1.1, significantly increased the binding affinity of BA.2S RBD to human ACE2
the L368I substitution augmented ACE2 binding affinity
the F486S substitution significantly decreased ACE2 binding affinity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 RBD binds ACE2 with higher affinity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does higher ACE2 binding translate to increased infectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 pseudovirus shows greater infectivity than BA.2, with R346T, L368I, and V83A increasing infectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure pseudovirus infectivity in HOS-ACE2/TMPRSS2 cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 pseudovirus infectivity is 7.6-fold greater than BA.2. R346T (1.9-fold), L368I (2.2-fold), V83A (3.3-fold) increase infectivity; Y144del (0.18-fold) and G252V (0.54-fold) decrease it.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Infectivity findings motivate fusogenicity measurement.

evidence.quote_or_span: the infectivity of the XBB.1 pseudovirus was 7.6-fold greater than that of the BA.2 pseudovirus
two substitutions in the RBD, R346T (1.9-fold) and L368I (2.2-fold), significantly increased pseudovirus infectivity
a substitution in the NTD, V83A (3.3-fold), significantly increased pseudovirus infectivity
two substitutions in the NTD, Y144del (0.18-fold) and G252V (0.54-fold), significantly decreased pseudovirus infectivity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 pseudovirus is more infectious.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is XBB.1 S more fusogenic than BA.2 and BA.2.75?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 S is more fusogenic than BA.2 and BA.2.75 despite lower surface expression, with V83A, R346T, and N460K contributing.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform cell-based fusion assay with S proteins.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 S is 2.2-fold more fusogenic than BA.2 S and 1.5-fold more than BA.2.75 S. V83A and R346T significantly increase fusogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Fusogenicity results prompt structural analysis to explain mechanisms.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 S is significantly more fusogenic than BA.2 S (2.2-fold) and BA.2.75 S (1.5-fold)
two substitutions, V83A and R346T, significantly increased fusogenicity
two substitutions in the RBD (R346T and N460K) and a substitution in the NTD (V83A) contribute to the augmented fusogenicity of XBB.1 S

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 S shows altered binding and fusogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the structural bases for altered binding and immune evasion?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cryo-EM structures show XBB.1 S predominantly in closed states, with F486S losing hydrophobic interactions with ACE2 and F490S altering interaction with ACE2 K31.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine cryo-EM structures of XBB.1 S ectodomain and S-ACE2 complex.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 S ectodomain reconstructed in two closed states at 2.50 Å and 2.51 Å. S-ACE2 complex at 3.29 Å. F486S loses hydrophobic interactions with ACE2.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structural insights inform in vitro replication and barrier disruption experiments.

evidence.quote_or_span: The XBB.1 S ectodomain was reconstructed as two closed states (closed-1 and closed-2) at resolutions of 2.50 Å and 2.51 Å
The XBB.1 S-ACE2 complex structures were reconstructed by cryo-EM analysis in both RBD one-up and two-up conformations
local refinement was performed on RBD-ACE2 and reconstructed at a resolution of 3.29 Å
the F486S substitution changed the side chain to a less bulky hydrophilic residue and lost hydrophobic interactions with the hydrophobic patch consisting of F28, L79, M82, and Y83 in ACE2
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 S is more fusogenic.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does XBB.1 replicate in vitro and affect epithelial barriers?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 growth kinetics are comparable to BA.2.75 in most cell lines but lower in iPSC-derived alveolar epithelial cells; XBB.1 disrupts airway epithelial-endothelial barriers more than BA.2.75.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform growth kinetics in various cell lines and airway-on-a-chip barrier disruption assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 less replicative than BA.2.75 in iPSC-derived alveolar epithelial cells. XBB.1 invasion into bottom channel of airway-on-chips significantly higher than BA.2.75.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: In vitro results lead to in vivo pathogenicity and transmission studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 was less replicative than BA.2.75 in this culture system (Fig. 5f).
the percentage of virus that invaded the bottom channel of XBB.1-infected airway-on-chips was significantly higher than that of BA.2.75-infected airway-on-chips (Fig. 5h).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 shows higher fusogenicity but lower pathogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why is there a discrepancy between fusogenicity and intrinsic pathogenicity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Discrepancy may be due to non-S mutations, theoretical limits, or hamster model differences.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Discuss limitations and propose explanations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Intrinsic pathogenicity of XBB.1 comparable or lower than BA.2.75. Non-S mutations may attenuate pathogenicity. Hamster model may not reproduce human condition.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight need for further studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: the intrinsic pathogenicity of XBB.1 was comparable or even lower than that of BA.2.75
certain mutations in the non-S region of the XBB.1 genome can attenuate viral pathogenicity augmented by the higher fusogenicity
a hamster model may not reproduce the human condition

it might be possible that the tropism and affinity of S proteins of these variants are different between human ACE2 and hamster ACE2, and therefore, a hamster model may not reproduce the human condition
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.

validation_methods: In vitro growth kinetics and airway-on-a-chip
success_verification.final_conclusion: XBB variant emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1, increasing fitness via recombination. XBB.1 exhibits profound immune resistance, enhanced ACE2 binding, increased infectivity and fusogenicity, but lower intrinsic pathogenicity in hamsters compared to Delta, comparable to BA.2.75. Discrepancy between fusogenicity and pathogenicity may be due to non-S mutations or model limitations.
limitations: Discrepancy between fusogenicity and intrinsic pathogenicity may be due to non-S mutations, theoretical limits, or hamster model differences.
Hamster model may not reproduce human condition due to potential differences in ACE2 tropism and affinity.

source.title: SESNet: sequence-structure feature-integrated deep learning method for data-efficient protein engineering

initial_request.research_problem: Predicting protein fitness for high-order mutants with limited experimental data.
initial_request.known_context: Deep learning has been widely used for protein engineering. However, it is limited by the lack of sufficient experimental data to train an accurate model for predicting the functional fitness of high-order mutants.
constraints: Limited experimental data available for training
quantifiable_goals: Achieve high Spearman correlation on fitness prediction across DMS datasets
Outperform state-of-the-art models on high-order mutant prediction

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Deep learning models for protein fitness prediction require large experimental datasets, which are scarce for high-order mutants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to accurately predict fitness of high-order mutants with limited experimental data?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Integrating sequence and structure information with an attention mechanism can improve prediction accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop a supervised deep-learning model (SESNet) that leverages local evolutionary context, global sequence features, and structure information.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Model architecture defined to integrate multiple information sources.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to benchmark the designed model against existing methods.

evidence.quote_or_span: Deep learning has been widely used for protein engineering. However, it is limited by the lack of sufficient experimental data to train an accurate model for predicting the functional fitness of high-order mutants.
we develop SESNet, a supervised deep-learning model to predict the fitness for protein mutants by leveraging both sequence and structure information, and exploiting attention mechanism.

Our model integrates local evolutionary context from homologous sequences, the global evolutionary context encoding rich semantic from the universal protein sequence space and the structure information accounting for the microenvironment around each residue in a protein.
we use Potts model to establish the local encoder

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SESNet architecture designed but not yet evaluated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does SESNet outperform existing models on DMS datasets?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SESNet will achieve state-of-the-art performance on fitness prediction across multiple DMS datasets.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Benchmark SESNet against supervised (ECNet) and unsupervised (ESM-1b, ESM-1v, MSA transformer) models on 20 DMS datasets.
evolution_trajectory.observation: SESNet achieves best performance in 19 out of 20 datasets and outperforms all models on high-order mutant prediction.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze contribution of each model component via ablation study.

evidence.quote_or_span: in 19 out of 20 datasets, the supervised models generally outperform the unsupervised ones as expected, and our model (SESNet) achieves the best performance among all the models.

we further explored the ability of our model to predict the fitness of higher-order variants by training it using the experimental results of the low-order variants on 6 datasets of DMS. As shown in Fig. 2B and C, our model outperforms all the other models.
we randomly split a given dataset into five folds by randomized shuffling and splitting.
We evaluated the performance for predicting the fitness of high-order mutants by the model trained on low-order mutants.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SESNet shows superior performance, but component contributions are unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which components of SESNet are most critical for performance?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: All three components (local, global, structure) contribute positively, with global encoder being most significant.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform ablation study by removing each component and measuring average Spearman correlation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Average Spearman: original 0.672, no local 0.639, no global 0.247, no structure 0.630. Global encoder most significant.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate attention weights to understand structure module's role.

evidence.quote_or_span: the average spearman correlation of the original model is 0.672, much higher than that without local encoder (0.639), that without global encoder (0.247) and that without structure module (0.630).

The ablation study reveals that all three components contribute to the improvement of model performance, and the contribution from the global encoder, which captures the sequence feature in global protein sequence universe, is the most significant.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Ablation study shows structure module contributes to performance, but its specific benefit is unclear.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the structure module improve identification of key residues?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Attention weights can identify key AA sites, and structure module enhances this identification.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze combined attention weights for GFP and RRM, with and without structure module.

parameters.key_site_definition: experimentally discovered top 20 sites or chromophore-forming AAs

evolution_trajectory.observation: 5/20 top attention-score sites are key for GFP, 11/20 for RRM. Without structure module, only 3 key residues in top-20 for GFP.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitation of structure module for precise structural information.

evidence.quote_or_span: The combined attention weights can be used to measure the importance of each AA site on protein fitness when mutated.
one can observe that, at least 5 out of 20 top attention-score A
The combined attention weights can be used to measure the importance of each AA site on protein fitness when mutated.
at least 5 out of 20 top attention-score AA sites predicted by our model are the key sites
Figure 3C and Additional file 1: Figure S2A show that 11 out of 20 top attention-score AA sites predicted by our model are the key AAs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Structure module improves key site identification but has limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the structure module sufficient for capturing precise structural information?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Current structure module may not fully capture structural details needed for optimizing fluorescence intensity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation based on GFP analysis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Structure module insufficient for capturing precise structural information for GFP fluorescence optimization.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Address data scarcity with a data augmentation strategy.

evidence.quote_or_span: The current structure module in our SESNet is not sufficient to fully capture such information and needs improvement.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SESNet performs well but requires large experimental data for training.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to achieve high accuracy with very limited experimental data (<50 points)?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pre-training on fitness scores from an unsupervised model can provide a good initialization, enabling effective fine-tuning with few experimental data.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose data augmentation: pre-train SESNet on ESM-IF1 predicted fitness for all single and 30,000 random double mutants, then fine-tune on limited experimental data.

parameters.pre-training_data: all single-site mutants + 30,000 random double-site mutants

evolution_trajectory.observation: Strategy designed to leverage unsupervised predictions for initialization.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the strategy on GFP dataset.

evidence.quote_or_span: we propose a data augmentation strategy by leveraging the data from unsupervised models to pre-train our model.

our model can achieve strikingly high accuracy in prediction of the fitness of protein mutants, especially for the higher order variants (>4 mutation sites), when finetuned by using only a small number of experimental mutation data (<50).

we propose a simple strategy of data augmentation by using the result generated by one unsupervised model to pre-train our model on a given protein, and then fine-tuning it using a limited number of experimental results on the same protein.
We used the fitness results of low-order mutants predicted by the unsupervised model, ESM-IF1, to pre-train our model.
we first built a mutant library, which contains all the single-site mutants and 30,000 double-site mutants randomly selected from tens of millions of saturated double-site mutants.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Data augmentation strategy designed but not tested.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does pre-training on unsupervised predictions improve performance with limited experimental data?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pre-trained SESNet will significantly outperform the original model when fine-tuned on small experimental datasets, especially for high-order mutants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Fine-tune pre-trained SESNet on 40 experimental data points from GFP and evaluate on multi-site mutants.

parameters.evaluation: Spearman correlation on multi-site mutants (2-8 sites)

evolution_trajectory.observation: Pre-trained model achieves Spearman 0.5–0.7 for multi-site mutants, outperforming original model and unsupervised model for high-order mutants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate strategy on other proteins and with another unsupervised model.

evidence.quote_or_span: the performance of the pre-trained model is significantly improved
the pretrained model can achieve high spearman correlation of 0.5–0.7 for multisite-mutants, even for high-order mutants with 5–8 mutation sites

when only 40 experimental data were used for training, the pretrained model has similar performance as compared to the unsupervised model for low-order mutants (<4 mutation sites), but clearly outperforms the latter for high-order mutants (>4 mutation sites).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Data augmentation successful on GFP with ESM-IF1.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the data augmentation strategy generalizable to other proteins and unsupervised models?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The strategy will work with other unsupervised models (ProGen2) and on other proteins (F7YBW8, CAPSD_AAV2S).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate using ProGen2 for pre-training and test on F7YBW8 (37 data points) and CAPSD_AAV2S (20 data points).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pre-trained model outperforms original and unsupervised models on multi-site mutants for both proteins.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Further validate on single-site mutants across many datasets.

evidence.quote_or_span: we used ProGen2 [8], an unsupervised model to learn the global sequence information, for data augmentation, and still derived the similar conclusion
To further validate the generality of the data augmentation strategy proposed here, we did the analysis on the dataset of other proteins

the pre-trained model outperforms both the original model without pretraining and the unsupervised model in the fitness prediction of all multi-site mutants (2–4 sites) after finetuned by using only 37 experimental data points
the pre-trained model also achieves the best performance in all of the high-order mutants ranging from 2 to 23 sites, when finetuned by only 20 experimental data points

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Data augmentation validated on multi-site mutants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does pre-training also improve single-site mutant prediction with limited data?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pre-trained model will outperform original model on single-site mutants across most datasets when fine-tuned on limited data.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Evaluate on 20 DMS datasets with 20, 40, or 100 experimental data points.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pre-trained model outperforms original in 18/20 datasets with 20 points, 19/20 with 40 or 100 points.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze why pre-training works via t-SNE visualization.

evidence.quote_or_span: our pre-trained model generally outperforms the original one in majority of datasets: 18 out of 20 datasets when fine-tuning on 20 experimental data points, and in 19 out of 20 datasets when finetuning on 40 or 100 experimental data points

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pre-training consistently improves performance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the mechanism behind the improvement from pre-training?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pre-training provides a good parameter initialization that separates positive and negative mutants in representation space.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform t-SNE visualization of representations from untrained, pre-trained, and fine-tuned models on GFP and RRM.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Untrained model cannot separate positive/negative variants; pre-training enables clear separation after fine-tuning.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude with overall success and limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: the representations from untrained model cannot provide a distinguishable boundary between positive and negative variants
the pre-training can furnish a good parameter initialization for SESNet

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SESNet architecture defined, but details of zero-shot score integration not specified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to incorporate zero-shot model scores into SESNet?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A dynamic weight (sigmoid gate) can control the influence of zero-shot scores, and MSE loss can be used for training.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Implement a sigmoid gate to dynamically weight zero-shot scores and use MSE loss for model training.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Model uses dynamic weight (sigmoid gate) to incorporate zero-shot scores, with MSE loss.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to training and evaluation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The scale of p quantifies how much should the model trust the score from the zero-shot model.
We utilize the mean square error (MSE) as the loss function to update model parameters during back-propagation

validation_methods: High-order mutant prediction trained on low-order data
Data augmentation with pre-training and fine-tuning
success_verification.final_conclusion: SESNet integrates sequence and structure information with attention, achieving state-of-the-art performance on 19/20 DMS datasets. A data augmentation strategy using unsupervised model pre-training enables high accuracy with very limited experimental data, especially for high-order mutants.
limitations: Structure module insufficient for capturing precise structural information for optimizing fluorescence intensity in GFP.

source.title: A computationally designed ACE2 decoy has broad efficacy against SARS-CoV-2 omicron variants and related viruses in vitro and in vivo

initial_request.research_problem: SARS-CoV-2 omicron subvariants evade monoclonal antibodies and vaccine immunity, necessitating broad-spectrum pan-coronavirus therapeutics.
initial_request.known_context: Omicron subvariants continue to mutate, reducing efficacy of existing antibodies and vaccines.
constraints: Must maintain broad neutralization across variants and sarbecoviruses
Must resist mutational escape
quantifiable_goals: Achieve picomolar binding affinity to RBD
Neutralize omicron subvariants in vitro and in vivo

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SARS-CoV-2 omicron subvariants evade monoclonal antibodies and vaccine immunity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for broad-spectrum pan-coronavirus therapeutics that resist mutational escape.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Affinity-enhanced soluble ACE2 decoys can outcompete native ACE2 and resist mutational escape because mutations reducing decoy binding would also reduce native receptor binding.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Pursue computational design of affinity-enhanced ACE2 decoys.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Any mutation in the RBD that limits binding to the sACE2 derivative will likely have reduced binding towards native ACE2 receptors potentially making the virus unfit to propagate.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hypothesis motivates design of first-generation decoy.

evidence.quote_or_span: SARS-CoV-2, especially B.1.1.529/omicron and its sublineages, continues to mutate to evade monoclonal antibodies and antibodies elicited by vaccination.
there is an urgent need to develop broad-spectrum pan-coronavirus therapeutics that can protect against both future SARS-CoV-2 variants and developing SARS-associated viruses
Any mutation in the RBD that limits binding to the sACE2 derivative will likely have reduced binding towards native ACE2 receptors potentially making the virus unfit to propagate.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis of affinity-enhanced ACE2 decoys.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to design a decoy with improved affinity and breadth.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations S19F, T27F, K31W, N330F will enhance binding.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design first-generation decoy FFWF using Rosetta flex ddG.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FFWF achieved 1.8 nM affinity but had limited coverage and solubility issues.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations of FFWF motivate second-generation design.

evidence.quote_or_span: Using computational protein design with the Rosetta flex ddG protocol $^{41}$ , we previously engineered a four mutation sACE2 decoy, FFWF, containing mutations S19F, T27F, K31W, and N330F with 1.
The S19F mutation due to its position on the ACE2 N-terminal periphery (limited interactions with the RBD) and K31W due to tryptophan's potential solubility issues were discarded

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FFWF decoy with 1.8 nM affinity but limited coverage and solubility issues.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to improve affinity and breadth while avoiding solubility problems.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Retaining T27F and N330F and adding Q42L and L79I will enhance binding and breadth.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design second-generation decoy FLIF using Rosetta Coupled Moves.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF designed with mutations T27F, Q42L, L79I, N330F.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: FLIF design enables computational binding prediction.

evidence.quote_or_span: We decided to include the T27F and N330F mutations in our second-generation ACE2 design
The S19F mutation due to its position on the ACE2 N-terminal periphery (limited interactions with the RBD) and K31W due to tryptophan's potential solubility issues were discarded
we chose mutation Q42L on ACE2 helix 1 due to its ability for leucine to form hydrophobic interactions with Y449-RBD

To create the second-generation ACE2 decoy, we chose mutation Q42L on ACE2 helix 1 due to its ability for leucine to form hydrophobic interactions with Y449-RBD and the mutation is spatially separated across the interface from mutations T27F and N330F (Fig.
The second-generation ACE2 decoy with 4 mutations (T27F, Q42L, L79I, and N330F) is referred to as FLIF throughout the manuscript.
Next, we analyzed the hydrogen bond network and native contacts for the four mutations (T27F, Q42L, L79I, N330F) important in our FLIF decoy within 3.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF designed with four mutations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to predict binding affinity of FLIF to Wuhan RBD.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CL-FEP can accurately predict absolute binding free energy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use CL-FEP to calculate FLIF-Wuhan RBD binding affinity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Predicted binding affinity -12.4 kcal/mol (~0.77 nM KD).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Strong predicted affinity supports experimental validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: FLIF-Wuhan RBD absolute binding affinity calculated using the CL-FEP approach
The reported values correspond to the mean and standard deviation among the 3 runs.
The calculated ABFE for FLIF:Wuhan RBD was -12.4 kcal/mol ( $\sim K_{D}$ 0.77 nM) (Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: CL-FEP prediction of FLIF binding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: MM/GBSA overestimates binding energy due to neglect of entropy.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CL-FEP provides more accurate absolute binding free energies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use CL-FEP instead of MM/GBSA for binding affinity predictions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: MM/GBSA overestimates binding energy; CL-FEP accounts for entropy and provides more accurate predictions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Accurate predictions guide experimental validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the MM/GBSA method overestimates the binding energy due to the disregard of entropy which is a major source of error and computational expense in MM/GBSA calculations

The conformational entropy change is usually computed by normal-mode analysis on a set of conformational snapshots taken from MD simulations. In this case, contribution from entropy is neglected because of its large computational cost and low prediction accuracy.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF designed and computationally predicted to bind tightly.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to experimentally measure binding affinity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FLIF will bind delta RBD with high affinity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure monovalent affinity of FLIF to delta RBD using BLI.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF bound delta RBD with KD 0.1 nM, ~80-fold tighter than WT ACE2 (8.2 nM).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Strong binding supports pseudovirus neutralization testing.

evidence.quote_or_span: we measured the monovalent affinity for soluble RBD from delta to wild type ACE2 and measured a $K_{D}$ of 8.2 nM (Fig. 2b) which is about 2-fold tighter than the measured $K_{D}$ of 16 nM for wild type RBD

we used BLI to measure the monovalent affinity between our FLIF decoy and delta RBD (Fig. 2c). The measured $K_{D}$ was 0.1 nM which constitutes an \~80-fold increase in binding compared with delta RBD:WT ACE2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF shows picomolar binding to delta RBD.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test neutralization against multiple SARS-CoV-2 variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FLIF will neutralize D614G, delta, BA.1, BA.2, BA.4/5 pseudoviruses with greater potency than WT ACE2.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test FLIF neutralization against pseudoviruses of D614G, delta, BA.1, BA.2, BA.4/5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF maintained potent neutralization against all tested variants, more efficacious than WT ACE2.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Potent neutralization against variants leads to testing against sarbecoviruses.

evidence.quote_or_span: the FLIF decoy maintained potent neutralization efficacy against the three subvariants of omicron tested at concentrations more efficacious than wild type ACE2
Therefore, we evaluated the efficacy of our FLIF decoy to neutralize SARS-CoV-2 S variants D614G, delta, omicron BA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF neutralizes SARS-CoV-2 variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test breadth against diverse sarbecoviruses.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FLIF will broadly neutralize sarbecoviruses.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test FLIF neutralization against SARS-CoV and sarbecoviruses WIV1, RsSHC014, GD-1, GX-P5L.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF neutralized SARS-CoV, GX-P5L, WIV1 with greater efficacy than WT ACE2; GD-1 and RsSHC014 with similar efficacy.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Broad neutralization validates FLIF is not over-engineered.

evidence.quote_or_span: FLIF neutralized SARS-CoV, GX-P5L (pangolin), and WIV1 (bat) with greater efficacy than wild type ACE2 and GD-1 (pangolin) and RsSHC014 (bat) with similar efficacy to wild type ACE2

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF shows broad in vitro neutralization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test in vivo efficacy against omicron BA.5.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FLIF will suppress viral RNA in lungs of BA.5-infected hamsters.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Administer FLIF (20 mg/kg) 2h post-infection in hamsters infected with BA.5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF suppressed viral RNA in lungs, while WT ACE2 did not.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: In vivo efficacy supports advantage over WT ACE2.

evidence.quote_or_span: viral RNA in the lungs of the hamsters was suppressed by treatment with FLIF but not by wild type ACE2
we verified the efficacy of our computationally designed ACE2 decoy, FLIF, against authentic omicron BA.5 in vitro and in vivo
to the best of our knowledge, no ACE2 decoy (computationally or experimentally engineered) has been tested in vivo against newer omicron subvariants such as BA.5

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF shows broad neutralization in vitro and in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm FLIF is not over-engineered and maintains breadth.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FLIF maintains breadth of neutralization across diverse sarbecoviruses and variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess breadth of neutralization data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF neutralized all sarbecoviruses tested, indicating it is not over-engineered at the expense of breadth.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validates design strategy.

evidence.quote_or_span: these results indicate that FLIF is not over-engineered at the expense of breadth as FLIF is able to neutralize all sarbecoviruses tested
FLIF can potently neutralize previous SARS-CoV-2 variants as well as current circulating variants such as BA.5, providing evidence that FLIF is not over engineered at the expense of breadth.
FLIF neutralized sarbecoviruses from both the SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 clades showcasing its promise as a pan-coronavirus therapeutic

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF shows in vivo efficacy against BA.5.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to demonstrate advantage of affinity-enhanced FLIF over WT ACE2.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Affinity-enhanced FLIF has a marked advantage over WT ACE2 in neutralizing BA.5 in vitro and in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare FLIF and WT ACE2 neutralization of BA.5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FLIF showed marked advantage over WT ACE2 in neutralizing BA.5 in vitro and in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Confirms affinity-enhanced design is beneficial.

evidence.quote_or_span: These data indicate that FLIF is efficacious at neutralizing omicron BA.5 in vitro and in vivo and affinity enhanced ACE2 mutants such as FLIF hold a major advantage at neutralizing omicron subvariants over the non-affinity enhanced wild type ACE2.
For the first time, we show that our computationally designed ACE2 decoy remains highly effective against the authentic SARS-CoV-2 omicron BA.5 strain in vitro and in vivo.
when compared to the efficacy of wild type ACE2 (i.e., non-affinity enhanced sACE2) engineered ACE2 decoys such as FLIF provide a marked advantage at neutralizing omicron BA.5 in vitro and in vivo.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FLIF demonstrated in vivo efficacy against BA.5 in hamsters.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: In vivo studies limited by attenuated disease in hamsters and early treatment time point (2h post-infection).
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The in vivo model may not fully recapitulate human infection, limiting generalizability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations of the hamster model and treatment timing.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Omicron variants produce attenuated disease in hamsters, and FLIF was administered 2-h post-infection, which may not mimic human treatment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight need for future studies with more virulent variants and later treatment time points.

evidence.quote_or_span: it is widely noted that omicron variants produce severely attenuated disease in mice and hamsters
we were not able to investigate whether FLIF improves the pathogenicity of hamsters infected with more virulent variants of SARS-CoV-2
FLIF was administered 2-h post-infection which may not mimic treatment of infection in humans.

validation_methods: Pseudovirus neutralization assays against multiple variants and sarbecoviruses
In vivo hamster model with BA.5
success_verification.final_conclusion: FLIF is a broad-spectrum pan-coronavirus therapeutic with picomolar affinity and in vivo efficacy against omicron BA.5, showing marked advantage over WT ACE2.
limitations: In vivo studies limited by attenuated disease in hamsters and early treatment time point (2h post-infection).

source.title: Virtual screening–based discovery of AI-2 quorum sensing inhibitors that interact with an allosteric hydrophobic site of LsrK and their functional evaluation

initial_request.research_problem: Existing LsrK inhibitors are limited, necessitating discovery of novel AI-2 QS inhibitors targeting LsrK.
initial_request.known_context: LsrK is a key kinase in the AI-2 quorum sensing pathway. Reported ATP-competitive LsrK inhibitors have limitations such as poor activity, low ligand efficiency, and ambiguous SARs.
constraints: Target the ATP-binding site of LsrK
Compounds should be drug-like
quantifiable_goals: Identify LsrK inhibitors with IC50 < 50 µM
Demonstrate AI-2 QS inhibition in cell-based assay
Confirm direct binding to LsrK

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Existing LsrK inhibitors have poor activity and unclear SARs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for new LsrK inhibitors with improved potency and defined binding modes.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeting the ATP-binding site of LsrK, particularly an allosteric hydrophobic pocket, can yield potent and selective inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use MD simulations to identify key interactions and an allosteric pocket, then perform virtual screening with constraints.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The ATP-binding site has an allosteric pocket that can become larger and be occupied by small molecule compounds.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design an integrated workflow to test the hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: research on it is limited and mostly aided by computer-aided drug design

these reported ATP-competitive LsrK inhibitors have limitations such as poor activity, low ligand efficiency, and ambiguous structure–activity relationships (SARs). Therefore, it is necessary to find new active and druggable LsrK inhibitors to combat antibiotic resistance.
the ATP-binding site has an allosteric pocket that can become larger and be occupied by small molecule compounds

we first identified key interactions between LsrK and ATP using MD simulations then performed in silico virtual screening of large-scale commercial compound libraries using a glide-based docking approach with constrained search criteria.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated; need to design experimental workflow.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to efficiently screen for LsrK inhibitors targeting the allosteric pocket?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: An integrated workflow of MD simulations, virtual screening, and bioassays can identify potent LsrK inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a workflow integrating MD simulations, virtual screening, LsrK inhibition assays, cell-based QS interference assays, and SPR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Workflow designed to screen for potential LsrK kinase inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform MD simulation to understand ATP binding and identify key residues.

evidence.quote_or_span: We designed a workflow integrating molecular dynamic (MD) simulations, virtual screening, LsrK inhibition assays, cell-based AI-2-mediated QS interference assays, and surface plasmon resonance (SPR)-based protein affinity assays to screen for potential LsrK kinase inhibitors.
we developed a workflow for the virtual screening and bioassay-based evaluation of AI-2 QSIs against ATP binding site of LsrK.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Workflow designed; need structural insights into LsrK-ATP binding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which residues are critical for ATP binding? Is there an allosteric pocket?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MD simulation of LsrK/ATP complex will reveal key binding residues and an allosteric hydrophobic pocket.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run 1000 ns MD simulation of LsrK/ATP complex.
evolution_trajectory.observation: MD revealed hydrogen bonds and salt bridges with Lys431, Tyr341, Arg319, Arg322; an allosteric hydrophobic pocket enlarges due to Glu345 deflection.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use key residues and pocket information to design virtual screening constraints.

evidence.quote_or_span: MD simulation results of the LsrK/ATP complex revealed hydrogen bonds and salt bridge formation among four key residues, namely, Lys 431, Tyr 341, Arg 319, and Arg 322, which are critical for the binding of ATP to LsrK.
MD simulation results indicated that the ATP-binding site has an allosteric pocket that can become larger and be occupied by small molecule compounds.

the conformation of ATP and the amino acid residues of the ATP binding site underwent significant changes, and there were two different binding modes between ATP and LsrK according to the drastic change in ATP RMSD values during MD simulations.
the deflection of the Glu 345 residue led to an increase in the volume of the hydrophobic pocket
a constraint search condition was added to form at least one hydrogen bond with the four key residues Arg319, Arg322, Tyr 341, and Lys 431
residues Arg 319, Arg 322, Tyr341, and Lys431 made significant contributions to the binding free energy

source.title: Optimal decision-making in high-throughput virtual screening pipelines

initial_request.research_problem: Optimizing resource allocation in high-throughput virtual screening (HTVS) pipelines to maximize return-on-computational-investment (ROCI).
initial_request.known_context: Enormous search space and high computational cost of high-fidelity models make screening practically challenging; existing HTVS pipelines rely on expert intuition without systematic ROCI optimization.
constraints: Linear pipeline structure
Fixed computational budget or joint optimization of throughput and efficiency
input_data: Joint score distribution of multi-fidelity models
quantifiable_goals: Maximize number of desirable candidates detected under budget constraint
Optimize trade-off between throughput and efficiency

evolution_trajectory.state_before: HTVS pipelines are constructed heuristically without systematic ROCI optimization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to optimally allocate computational resources across multi-fidelity models to maximize ROCI.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimally allocating computational resources to models with varying costs and accuracy can optimize ROCI.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose a general framework for constructing and optimizing an HTVS pipeline with multi-fidelity models, optimizing screening thresholds based on joint score distribution.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Framework formulated to predict optimal screening policy ψ* = [λ1*, λ2*, ..., λN-1*] that maximizes reward under resource constraints.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate framework on synthetic data with known joint distribution.

evidence.quote_or_span: the enormous search space containing the candidates and the substantial computational cost of high-fidelity property prediction models make screening practically challenging

the construction of such HTVS pipelines and the strategies for operating them heavily rely on expert intuition, often resulting in heuristic methods without systematic considerations for increasing the return-on-computational-investment (ROCI)
The central idea is to optimally allocate the computational resources to models with varying costs and accuracy to optimize the return-on-computational-investment (ROCI).
we propose a general framework for constructing and optimizing a virtual screening (HTVS) pipeline that consists of multi-fidelity models

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Framework proposed but untested.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to validate framework on a controlled synthetic pipeline.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A synthetic 4-stage pipeline with known multivariate Gaussian joint distribution can demonstrate optimization benefits.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design synthetic 4-stage HTVS pipeline with known joint distribution, costs 1,10,100,1000, 100 true candidates out of 10^5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Synthetic pipeline constructed with stages S1-S4, costs increasing, and known joint Gaussian distribution.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Vary correlation to study impact on optimized performance.

evidence.quote_or_span: we consider a synthetic HTVS pipeline with 4 stages (i.e., N = 4), where the joint PDF of the predictive scores from all stages is assumed to be known.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Synthetic pipeline designed with fixed correlation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does correlation between stages affect optimization?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Varying correlation ρ (0.8 high, 0.3 low) will reveal impact on optimized pipeline performance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run experiments with ρ=0.8 and ρ=0.3.
evolution_trajectory.observation: High correlation (ρ=0.8) yields better performance; low correlation (ρ=0.3) reduces performance but still better than random.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze quantitative results for high correlation case.

evidence.quote_or_span: We vary the correlation levels between the scores from neighboring stages to investigate the overall impact on the performance of the optimized HTVS pipeline.
8$ , where the neighboring stages yield scores that are highly correlated, and next, for $\rho = 0.3$ where the correlation is very low.
We validate the proposed HTVS optimization framework for two cases: first, for $\rho = 0.8$ , where the neighboring stages yield scores that are highly correlated, and next, for $\rho = 0.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experiments run for high correlation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the quantitative benefit of optimization under budget constraint?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimized pipeline will detect most candidates at fraction of cost.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compute detection rate vs. cost for high correlation case.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Optimized four-stage pipeline detected 97% of desirable candidates at 10% of total computational cost.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Derive insight on optimal stage ordering.

evidence.quote_or_span: the optimized four-stage pipeline detected 97% of the desirable candidates that meet the target criterion at only 10% of the total computational cost

evolution_trajectory.state_before: High correlation results obtained.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the best strategy for ordering stages?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Placing stages in increasing order of complexity is optimal when correlations are high.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze impact of stage ordering.
evolution_trajectory.observation: When all stages are highly correlated, best strategy is to place stages in increasing order of complexity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Check robustness across configurations.

evidence.quote_or_span: when all stages are relatively highly correlated to each other, the best strategy for constructing the HTVS pipeline appears to place the stages in increasing order of complexity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Low correlation experiment completed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does optimization still help with low correlation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multi-stage optimized pipeline still provides better trade-off than random sampling.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare optimized pipeline performance against random sampling for ρ=0.3.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Multi-stage HTVS pipeline with optimized screening policy still provides much better trade-off between computational cost and detection performance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Combine with high correlation results to assess overall robustness.

evidence.quote_or_span: the multi-stage HTVS pipeline with the optimized screening policy still provides a much better trade-off between the computational cost for screening and the detection performance.
8$ , where the neighboring stages yield scores that are highly correlated, and next, for $\rho = 0.3$ where the correlation is very low.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Results from both correlation levels available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the framework robust across different pipeline configurations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Framework significantly improves performance across all configurations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Evaluate performance across all pipeline configurations in Table 1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Proposed HTVS pipeline optimization framework significantly improved overall screening performance across all pipeline configurations in a highly robust manner.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply framework to real-world lncRNA screening problem.

evidence.quote_or_span: our proposed HTVS pipeline optimization framework was able to significantly improve the overall screening performance across all pipeline configurations in a highly robust manner.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No HTVS pipeline exists for fast and reliable screening of lncRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to construct an lncRNA HTVS pipeline and estimate joint score distribution.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A pipeline with four algorithms (CPC2, CPAT, PLEK, LncFinder) in increasing complexity can be optimized using the framework.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct lncRNA HTVS pipeline with four stages; estimate joint distribution via EM on 4% training data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pipeline constructed; joint distribution estimated as multivariate Gaussian mixture via EM; correlation heatmap shows CPAT highest correlation to LncFinder, PLEK lower.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Evaluate performance of optimized lncRNA HTVS pipelines.

evidence.quote_or_span: no HTVS pipeline has been proposed to date for fast and reliable screening of lncRNAs

we performed a spectral estimation under the assumption that the joint score distribution follows a multivariate Gaussian mixture model and estimated the parameters via the expectation-maximization (EM) scheme

Figure 3: The heat map showing Pearson's correlation coefficient between different stages. CPAT had the highest correlation to LncFinder. While PLEK was computationally more complex compared to CPAT, it showed a relatively lower correlation to LncFinder.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: lncRNA pipeline constructed and joint distribution estimated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the performance of jointly optimized pipelines for throughput and efficiency?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimized pipeline will detect most lncRNAs with significant computational savings.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Evaluate performance of lncRNA HTVS pipelines jointly optimized for throughput and efficiency with α=0.5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: On average, optimized HTVS pipeline detected 48,372 lncRNAs out of 50,266 total lncRNAs in test dataset.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze robustness to stage inclusion and ordering.

evidence.quote_or_span: On average, the optimized HTVS pipeline detected 48, 372 lncRNAs out of 50, 266 total lncRNAs in the test dataset.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Performance results obtained for various pipeline configurations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does inclusion of less correlated stage (PLEK) degrade performance? Does ordering matter?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimal threshold selection mitigates impact of less correlated stages; ordering does not significantly affect performance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze effect of including PLEK and varying stage order.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Inclusion of less correlated stage does not significantly degrade average screening performance; average performance does not significantly depend on actual ordering of stages when thresholds are optimized; with all four stages, optimized pipeline detected 48,402 lncRNAs on average with computational savings 42.27% to 44.00%.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate against baseline approach.

evidence.quote_or_span: the inclusion of a less correlated stage in the HTVS pipeline does not significantly degrade the average screening performance.

the proposed optimization framework enables one to select the optimal threshold values that can sensibly combine the benefits of the most computationally efficient stages (such as CPC2 and CPAT in this case) as well as the highest fidelity stage (LncFinder)
the average performance does not significantly depend on the actual ordering of the stages when the screening threshold values are optimized via our proposed framework.
when using all four stages in the HTVS pipeline (N = 4), the optimized pipeline detected 48, 402 lncRNAs on average and with consistent computational savings ranging between 42.27% and 44.00%.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Optimized pipeline performance characterized.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does optimized pipeline compare to baseline in terms of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and cost?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Optimized pipeline will identify most true lncRNAs at reduced cost but may have lower specificity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare optimized pipeline against baseline approach using CPAT and LncFinder.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All optimized pipelines including CPAT identified at least 91% of true lncRNAs at 50% of original computational cost; optimized pipeline resulted in lower specificity compared to baseline.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations and future work.

evidence.quote_or_span: All the optimized pipelines including CPAT which is reasonably correlated to LncFinder identified at least 91% of the true lncRNAs at 50% of the original computational cost.
The optimized pipeline resulted in lower SP compared to the baseline.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Framework validated on synthetic and real data.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Framework assumes linear pipeline structure; real pipelines may be DAG or require dynamic policies.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Future work should extend framework to non-linear structures and dynamic policies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify limitations and propose future research directions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Framework currently limited to linear pipelines; future work needed for DAG structures and reinforcement learning-based dynamic policies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude study.

evidence.quote_or_span: how can we design an optimal screening policy when the structure of the HTVS pipeline is not linear (i.e., sequential)?
It would be interesting to investigate how one may design optimal screening policies for pipelines whose structure is a directed acyclic graph (DAG).
we can envision designing a dynamic screening policy—e.g., based on reinforcement learning—where there is no predetermined “pipeline structure”

validation_methods: Synthetic pipeline experiments with known ground truth
Real-world lncRNA screening with baseline comparison
metrics: Detection rate (percentage of true candidates identified)
success_verification.final_conclusion: The proposed optimization framework significantly improves ROCI in HTVS pipelines, achieving high detection rates (e.g., 97% at 10% cost in synthetic, ≥91% at 50% cost in real lncRNA) with robust performance across configurations, though specificity may be lower than baseline.
limitations: Assumes linear pipeline structure; not applicable to DAG or dynamic pipelines
Lower specificity compared to baseline in real-world validation
Joint distribution estimation may be challenging for complex pipelines

source.title: Unlocking potential inhibitors for Bruton's tyrosine kinase through in-silico drug repurposing strategies

initial_request.research_problem: Identify potential BTK inhibitors through in-silico drug repurposing to overcome limitations of existing inhibitors.
initial_request.known_context: BTK inhibition is a therapeutic strategy for complex diseases, but existing inhibitors like ibrutinib have side effects and selectivity issues.
constraints: Use FDA-approved drug library
In-silico only
input_data: FDA-approved drug library from DrugBank (~3000 compounds)
quantifiable_goals: Identify compounds with binding affinity ≤ -10.1 kcal/mol
Ligand efficiency > 0.30 kcal/mol/non-H atom

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Existing BTK inhibitors like ibrutinib have side effects and selectivity issues.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for small molecule BTK inhibitors with low cytotoxicity and high efficacy.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Drug repurposing can identify potential BTK inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Adopt drug repurposing approach to screen FDA-approved drugs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Drug repurposing has emerged as a promising approach for identifying potential inhibitors of BTK.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design a computational pipeline for virtual screening and evaluation.

evidence.quote_or_span: inhibition of BTK has been proposed as a therapeutic strategy for various complex diseases
it is crucial to find small molecule inhibitors of BTK that have low cytotoxicity and high efficacy
drug repurposing has emerged as a promising approach for identifying potential inhibitors of BTK

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that drug repurposing can identify BTK inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a systematic computational pipeline to screen and evaluate candidates.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Molecular docking-based virtual screening followed by MD simulations, PCA, and MM-PBSA can identify and validate BTK inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a multi-step in-silico pipeline: virtual screening, MD simulations, PCA, MM-PBSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pipeline designed: virtual screening of FDA-approved drugs, then MD simulations, PCA, and MM-PBSA for top hits.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Execute virtual screening of ~3000 FDA-approved drugs.

evidence.quote_or_span: We performed a molecular docking-based virtual screening using a library of FDA-approved drugs.

We then used all-atom molecular dynamics (MD) simulations followed by principal component analysis (PCA) and Molecular Mechanics Poisson Boltzmann Surface Area (MM-PBSA) to further evaluate the top-ranking compounds' binding interactions and stability.
The results of the docking study indicate that approximately 3000 drugs were screened for their ability to bind to the target protein BTK.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pipeline designed; ready for virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which FDA-approved drugs bind BTK with high affinity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Virtual screening will identify compounds with strong binding affinity to BTK.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform molecular docking-based virtual screening of ~3000 FDA-approved drugs against BTK (PDB: 5P9J).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Approximately 3000 drugs screened for binding to BTK.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze docking results to select top hits.

evidence.quote_or_span: approximately 3000 drugs were screened for their ability to bind to the target protein BTK
An FDA-approveddrug library was downloaded from the DrugBank database

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Virtual screening completed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select most promising compounds based on binding affinity and ligand efficiency.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Top hits with binding affinity ≤ -10.1 kcal/mol and high ligand efficiency are promising BTK inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select top five hits with binding affinity ≤ -10.1 kcal/mol; further prioritize Eltrombopag and Alectinib based on ligand efficiency >0.30.

tool_or_method.name: binding affinity and ligand efficiency analysis

evolution_trajectory.observation: Two compounds, Eltrombopag and Alectinib, have significant LE values (>0.30 kcal/mol/non-H atom).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze interactions of Eltrombopag and Alectinib with BTK.

evidence.quote_or_span: two (Eltrombopag and Alectinib) of the selected hits have significant LE values (>0.30 kcal/mol/non-H atom)
two (Eltrombopag and Alectinib) of the selected hits have significant LE values (>0.30 kcal/mol/non-H atom) with BTK

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Eltrombopag and Alectinib selected as top hits.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do these compounds interact with BTK?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Both drugs bind the ATP-binding site, interacting with key residues like Lys430, Cys481, Asp539.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform interaction analysis of docked poses.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Both drugs interact with ATP-binding site; Eltrombopag forms H-bonds with Lys430 (3.2, 3.4 Å), Ser538 (3.3, 3.1 Å), Asp539 (3.1, 3.1 Å); Alectinib with Ser538 (3.3 Å).

source.title: Hierarchical Virtual Screening of Potential New Antibiotics from Polyoxygenated Dibenzofurans against Staphylococcus aureus Strains

initial_request.research_problem: Identify new antibacterial agents from symmetrical polyoxygenated dibenzofurans against MRSA using hierarchical virtual screening.
initial_request.known_context: Antimicrobial resistance is a top global health threat; MRSA caused >100,000 deaths in 2019. Polyoxygenated dibenzofurans like achyrofuran and rhodomyrtoxin B have shown antibacterial activity, but structure-activity relationships are not conclusively established.
constraints: Purely computational study; no experimental validation performed.
input_data: Tetrahydroxybenzofuran (pivot) and nine selected molecules with known MIC values
quantifiable_goals: Identify molecules with better predicted binding affinity than methicillin and oxacillin
Select candidates with favorable ADME/Tox and synthetic accessibility

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: There is no conclusive information about the structure-activity relationships of dibenzofurans.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Use a tetrahydroxybenzofuran pivot to build a pharmacophore model for virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Known dibenzofurans like achyrofuran and rhodomyrtoxin B show antibacterial activity, but SAR is unclear.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: A pharmacophore model is needed to capture essential features for antibacterial activity.

there is still no conclusive information about the properties associated with the structure–activity relationships of dibenzofurans

evidence.quote_or_span: The best alignment score was 32.476

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pharmacophore model available.
