evolution_trajectory.observation: Downloaded 20 most informative combinations, replaced LacI plasmid with purified LacI, performed experiment as Day_0.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the 20 setups with purified LacI.

evidence.quote_or_span: Downloaded 20 most informative combinations
Replaced LacI plasmid with purified LacI
Performed the experiment, input to the model as Day_0
Using a module of METIS called “K most informative combinations” (with the number K to be defined by the user), we extracted the 20 most informative combinations of the active learning cycle
repeated these 20 setup by replacing $P_{T7}-LacI$ plasmid with purified LacI protein (Fig. 3j), resulting in a strong improvement in the objective function, and in particular Gfp fold-change

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 20 setups with purified LacI designed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Will replacing LacI plasmid with purified LacI improve Gfp fold-change?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Purified LacI protein will result in strong improvement in objective function and Gfp fold-change.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Repeat the 20 setups by replacing P_T7-LacI plasmid with purified LacI protein.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strong improvement in objective function and Gfp fold-change.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that resource competition was limiting for all SLC and MLC circuits.

evidence.quote_or_span: To overcome resource competition, we tested purified LacI protein instead of the LacI plasmid in the TXTL system, which resulted in improved Gfp productivity
repeated these 20 setup by replacing $P_{T7}-LacI$ plasmid with purified LacI protein (Fig. 3j), resulting in a strong improvement in the objective function, and in particular Gfp fold-change

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Improvement observed with purified LacI.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Was resource competition the limiting factor for all circuits?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: All SLC and MLC circuits will improve upon providing external LacI.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Check improvement of all four SLC and MLC circuits with external LacI.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All four SLC and MLC circuits improved upon providing external LacI, confirming resource competition as limiting factor.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Continue with one additional round of active learning using the new dataset.

evidence.quote_or_span: Note that among these 20 combinations were again all four SLC and MLC circuits. All of them improved upon providing external LacI, clearly demonstrating that resource competition had been limiting performance of the SLC and MLC circuits.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Resource competition validated as limiting factor.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can further active learning improve fold-change?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: One additional round of active learning with the new dataset will improve fold-change up to 123.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Continue with one additional round of active learning using the dataset from the purified LacI experiment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Fold-change improved to up to 123, a 15-fold improvement over 10 rounds with plasmid and 34-fold over initial.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply workflow to optimize transcription & translation unit.

evidence.quote_or_span: Continuing with only one additional round of active learning using this dataset, we were able to improve the fold-change to up to 123 (Fig. 3k), which is 15-fold improvement compared to that of 10 rounds of active learning with the $P_{T7}-LacI$ plasmid and 34-fold improvement compared to the initial setup.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LacI circuit optimization completed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can METIS optimize a transcription & translation unit with many potential conditions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Active learning can achieve 12-fold improvement in yield with only 50 transcription & translation units tested per round over 4 rounds.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Optimize transcription & translation unit with 10,800 potential conditions using 50 units per round for 4 rounds.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Yield improved up to 12-fold on Day 3.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze effect of exploration rate on optimization.

evidence.quote_or_span: The plot representing the average of triplicates (n = 3 independent experiments) as the result of 4 rounds of active learning, with 50 transcription & translation units tested per round.
Over 4 rounds of active learning, yield of the transcription & translation unit improved up to 12-fold on Day 3.

evidence.quote_or_span: Using a high exploration rate on Day 3 resulted in a wide distribution of yields, but no further improvement, indicating that an optimum had been reached
we additionally investigated the effect of the 20 most informative combinations in vivo

source.title: Therapeutic enzyme engineering using a generative neural network

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering therapeutic enzymes for mRNA therapeutics is challenging due to vast sequence space and inaccurate or labor-intensive mapping methods. The goal is to improve expression, half-life, and catalytic efficiency of ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) while minimizing mutations to reduce immunogenicity.
initial_request.known_context: Sequence space is vast; computational and experimental mapping methods are inaccurate or time-/labor-intensive.
constraints: Minimize mutations to reduce immunogenicity
Maintain or improve expression and half-life
input_data: 3818 OTC homolog sequences from BLAST search
quantifiable_goals: Improve specific activity relative to hOTC
Improve thermal stability (melting temperature)
Maintain or improve expression in mammalian cells

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Vast sequence space and inaccurate/labor-intensive mapping methods hinder therapeutic enzyme engineering.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Existing methods like consensus design only consider position- or pair-wise conservation, missing higher-order interactions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Capturing higher-order co-evolutionary relationships with a generative model (VAE) will yield more functional variants than consensus approaches.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use a variational autoencoder (VAE) to model sequence co-evolution and sample near-human OTC variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The most effective sequence design methods may need to consider interactions between all positions in a sequence alignment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design and train a VAE on OTC homolog sequences to test the hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: sequence space is incomprehensibly vast, and methods to map sequence to function (computationally or experimentally) are inaccurate or time-/labor-intensive.

the most effective sequence design methods may need to consider not just position- or pair-wise conservation when making mutations, but also the interactions between all positions in a given sequence alignment.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that VAE can capture higher-order interactions; need to build and train model.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to construct a VAE that can learn from OTC homolog sequences and generate near-human variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A convolutional VAE trained on 3818 OTC homologs with weighted sampling favoring human-like sequences will generate functional near-human variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build VAE with two convolutional neural networks (encoder and decoder), latent dimension 64, and train with weighted sampling based on similarity to hOTC.

parameters.weighted_sampling: inversely proportional to Hamming distance to hOTC

evolution_trajectory.observation: VAE designed and trained; weighted sampling addresses class imbalance from microbial bias in databases.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that the trained VAE accurately captures sequence statistics.

evidence.quote_or_span: we applied variational autoencoders (VAEs), a recent advance from the deep learning literature
we built a large multi-sequence alignment of OTC homologs obtained by BLAST search. After filtering these sequences to remove those that caused the most gaps, we were left with 3818 sequences
Weighted sampling with replacement was used to preferentially select training data for each batch based on the similarity to hOTC.
Our VAE consists of two convolutional neural networks (an encoder and a decoder).
The number of filters in the encoder blocks were 128, 96, and 64 respectively, and the outputs were pooled two-fold in the length dimension only.
We used a latent space of dimension 64.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: VAE trained with weighted sampling; need to verify it captures evolutionary statistics.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the VAE accurately reproduce site-wise amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information from the training data?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: If the VAE has learned the distribution, generated samples should match training data statistics.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare per-position amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information between VAE samples and training data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Site-wise amino acid frequencies and pairwise mutual information match training data with Pearson's R^2 of 0.99.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to generate a library of near-human OTC variants using the validated VAE.

evidence.quote_or_span: Per position amino acid frequency in VAE samples match those in the training data.
scatter plot of all amino acid frequencies (training vs. samples), (Pearson's $R^{2}$ value of 0.99).
Second-order correlations in VAE samples match those in the training data.
a scatter plot of position-to-position mutual information between the training data and the samples (Pearson's $R^{2}$ value of 0.99).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validated VAE model; need to generate a library of variants for experimental testing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to sample near-human variants from the VAE that are likely functional?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Encoding hOTC and sampling from its latent distribution will yield variants close to human with multiple substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Encode hOTC, sample repeatedly from its encoded distribution, and remove duplicates to create a library of 87 unique near-hOTC variants.

parameters.sampling_method: encode hOTC, sample from latent distribution

evolution_trajectory.observation: Generated 87 unique near-hOTC variants with average >98% identity and 8 substitutions on average, spanning 32 distinct locations.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Build a consensus library as a control for comparison.

evidence.quote_or_span: This method yielded 87 unique near-hOTC variants (with an average of $>98\%$ and no less than 95% identity) with mutations spanning the length of the gene at 32 distinct locations (Fig. 2B)
The variants on average contained 8 amino acid substitutions relative to hOTC (Fig. 2C)
To sample near-human variants from the VAE for testing, we first encoded the hOTC sequence, resulting in a set of means and variances.
After removing duplicates, this resulted in the 87 variants tested in this work.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: VAE library generated; need a control library for comparison.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a baseline to assess whether VAE-designed variants outperform traditional consensus design.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A consensus library incorporating 18 amino acid differences from hOTC will serve as a control.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify 18 consensus amino acid differences, build a fully combinatorial library of ~10^7 variants, and randomly select 91 unique variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Consensus library of 91 unique variants created with 18 amino acid differences from hOTC.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Experimentally screen both libraries for growth rescue in E. coli auxotroph.

evidence.quote_or_span: we identified 18 amino acid differences between the hOTC wildtype sequence and the consensus
We encoded these amino acid substitutions into a fully combinatorial library of $\sim10^{7}$ variants and randomly selected 91 unique variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: VAE and consensus libraries ready; need to assess functionality in a cellular context.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do the designed variants retain OTC function in vivo?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants from both libraries will rescue growth of an E. coli arginine auxotroph, with VAE variants potentially outperforming consensus.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Transform E. coli arginine auxotroph with each variant and measure growth (OD600) after 8 hours.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ~96% of variants (84/87 VAE, 87/91 consensus) outperformed hOTC, with OD600 increases of 1.5–2.5-fold.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize all variants biochemically for specific activity and thermal stability.

evidence.quote_or_span: almost all VAE (84/87, orange) and consensus (87/91, blue) variants outperformed hOTC (green), promoting OD600 increases of 1.5–2.5-fold
Initial growth curves (Figure S3) showed that cultures hit logarithmic phase after 6–8 h of growth at 37 °C, 1500 rpm.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Growth rescue confirmed; need to quantify specific activity and thermal stability of all variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do VAE and consensus libraries compare in terms of specific activity and thermostability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: VAE library will show higher mean specific activity and improved thermostability compared to consensus.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Recombinantly produce all OTC variants, measure specific activity and melting temperature (Tm) via differential scanning fluorimetry.
evolution_trajectory.observation: VAE library shows 1.4-fold higher mean activity (p<0.0001) vs consensus 0.96-fold; VAE Tm improvement 12°C vs consensus 8°C; all VAE variants improved thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze the distribution of variants in activity-stability space.

evidence.quote_or_span: all OTC variants (i.e., both VAE and consensus variants) were recombinantly produced, and activity and thermostability were characterized

comparison of the mean activity between the two libraries (solid black lines) indicates a statistically significant difference (p value <0.0001); the consensus library on average has a mean activity that is 0.96-fold relative to hOTC while the VAE library shows a significant improvement with a mean activity of 1.4-fold relative to hOTC.
all VAE variants exhibited improved thermostability with respect to hOTC

With respect to thermal stability, both consensus and VAE approaches yielded libraries with substantially elevated average melting temperatures: a 8 C° improvement for the consensus library and a 12 C° improvement for the VAE library.
The average melting temperature ( $T_{m}$ ) of the VAE library was 73 °C (solid black line), a 12 °C improvement over hOTC.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Activity and stability data collected; need to compare hit rates for improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which method yields more variants with both improved activity and stability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: VAE method will boost the hit rate in the upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and specific activity).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Plot variants in activity vs. stability space and compare quadrant distributions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: VAE method boosts hit rate in upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and specific activity) compared to consensus, which yields more variants with improved stability but decreased activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test top VAE variants in mammalian cells to assess therapeutic potential.

evidence.quote_or_span: the most striking difference between the two methods is seen when comparing the two quadrants in the upper half of the panel. The consensus approach yields more variants in the upper left quadrant (improved thermostability, but decreased specific activity), while the VAE method boosts the hit rate in the upper right quadrant (improved thermostability and improved specific activity).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Top VAE variants identified; need to evaluate performance in a mammalian system relevant to mRNA therapeutics.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Will the improved specific activity translate to mammalian cells despite potential expression differences?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Codon-optimized mRNAs of top VAE variants will show improved specific activity in HepG2 cells, even if expression levels vary.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Synthesize codon-optimized mRNAs for the 12 VAE variants with highest specific activity, transfect into HepG2 cells, and measure expression and activity.

parameters.measurement: OTC expression (western blot) and activity (citrulline formation)

evolution_trajectory.observation: Expression levels 0.5–0.75-fold of hOTC, but specific activities 1–1.5-fold greater; seven variants showed same or slightly improved total activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze mutation effects to understand contributions to activity and stability.

evidence.quote_or_span: the 12 VAE sequences with highest specific activity were synthesized as codon-optimized mRNAs for in vitro evaluation.
The 12 VAE OTC mRNAs were synthesized as described in Materials and Methods and transfected into HepG2 cells
OTC protein expression and activity were measured in total lysates one day after transfection.
Expression levels of the variants were on par or lower (0.5–0.75-fold) than that observed with hOTC
the specific activities of all OTC variants were 1–1.5-fold greater than that of hOTC

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Phenotypic data collected; need to dissect mutational effects and epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the contributions of individual mutations and epistatic interactions to activity and stability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Linear regression can estimate mutation effects, and comparison of library variances will reveal the role of epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform regularized linear regression on 182 variants with 48 predictors; compare activity and stability variances between libraries.
evolution_trajectory.observation: No global anti-correlation between activity and stability; VAE library's higher mean activity suggests epistatic contributions, but comparable variance indicates epistasis does not dominate; consensus mutations primarily affect stability at individual residue level, while residue-residue correlations constrain extreme stability values.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations and suggest future work.

evidence.quote_or_span: We used regularized least squares linear regression to estimate the effects of individual mutations on specific activity and thermostability.

Taken as a whole, however, specific activity and melting temperature do not appear to be globally anti-correlated, as might have been expected if the sequences were close to optimal with respect to both high specific activity and high melting temperature.
The enhanced mean activity observed for the VAE library suggests that the epistatic interactions captured in this library contribute to increased catalytic rate.

However, the observation that the VAE and consensus libraries exhibit comparable variance in activity suggests that the residues involved in epistatic interactions do not dominate in their contributions to catalytic activity.
Our observations include all 87 sampled VAE sequences and 85 of 90 consensus library sequences after some initial filtering to remove entries with null data $n = 182$ .

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Interpretation of epistasis and stability mechanisms; need to acknowledge study limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Limited number of variants tested; mechanistic basis not fully explored.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Further experiments and analysis are needed to fully investigate the physical/mechanistic basis of library mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation and suggest future work.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Limited number of variants tested; further experiments needed to fully investigate mechanistic basis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude study with validation summary.

evidence.quote_or_span: However, we were limited in the number of variants we tested, and further experiments and analysis will be needed to fully investigate the physical/mechanistic basis of library mutations on OTC stability and catalytic activity.

validation_methods: Differential scanning fluorimetry for thermal stability
Mammalian cell expression and activity assay
success_verification.final_conclusion: VAE-designed OTC variants show significantly improved specific activity (1.4-fold mean increase) and thermal stability (12°C Tm improvement) over hOTC, with a higher hit rate for combined improvements compared to consensus design. Top variants maintain improved specific activity in mammalian cells despite lower expression. Epistatic interactions captured by VAE contribute to activity, but individual residue effects dominate stability.
limitations: Limited number of variants tested (87 VAE, 91 consensus)
Mechanistic basis of mutations not fully investigated
Lower expression of VAE variants in mammalian cells

source.title: Parallel profiling of antigenicity alteration and immune escape of SARS-CoV-2 Omicron and other variants

initial_request.research_problem: SARS-CoV-2 variants, especially Omicron, accumulate RBD mutations that alter antigenicity and cause immune escape, threatening antibody therapies and vaccines.
initial_request.target_object: SARS-CoV-2 spike RBD mutations and their impact on antibody binding and neutralization
initial_request.known_context: Variants like Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, and Omicron carry mutations such as E484K, N501Y, K417N, L452R, T478K, and F490S that may reduce antibody efficacy.
constraints: Limited to antibodies with available RBD-bound crystal structures
Computational predictions limited by input structure resolution
quantifiable_goals: Identify mutations that most disrupt antibody binding (ΔΔG increase)
Validate computational predictions with in vitro binding and neutralization assays
Determine whether maximal binding or KD better predicts neutralization

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SARS-CoV-2 variants accumulate critical RBD mutations leading to antigenicity changes and immune escape, threatening antibody therapies and vaccines.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The impact of individual and combinatorial RBD mutations on antibody binding and neutralization is not systematically profiled.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Computational interface analysis (flexddG) can estimate mutation-driven immune escape and aid early surveillance of concerning variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a parallel computational and experimental study to profile mutations of Omicron and other variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The application of such methods for early surveillance of SARS-CoV-2 mutations may help estimate the effect of mutation-driven immune escape and adaptation during the clinical application of antibody-based antiviral therapies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to computational screening of mutations on antibody-RBD and ACE2-RBD complexes.

evidence.quote_or_span: SARS-CoV-2 variants have evolved a variety of critical mutations, leading to antigenicity changes and immune escape.
The recent emerging SARS-CoV-2 Omicron variant attracted global attention due to its significant resistance to current antibody therapies and vaccines.
Here, we profiled the mutations of Omicron and other various circulating SARS-CoV-2 variants in parallel by computational interface analysis and in vitro experimental assays.

The application of such methods for early surveillance of SARS-CoV-2 mutations may help estimate the effect of mutation-driven immune escape and adaptation during the clinical application of antibody-based antiviral therapies.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that flexddG can predict mutation impact on antibody binding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to computationally assess the effect of single and combinatorial RBD mutations on antibody and ACE2 binding.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations at key interface residues (E484, N501, K417, L452R, T478K, F490S) will increase ΔΔG, indicating reduced binding affinity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform flexddG calculations on a panel of antibody-RBD and ACE2-RBD complexes for single mutations and combinatorial sets.

parameters.combinatorial_sets: Omicron and other variant mutation sets

evolution_trajectory.observation: flexddG results revealed that mutations from SARS-CoV-2 variants diminished neutralizing antibody binding to varying degrees. E484 mutations caused the most significant ΔΔG increase; L452R and T478K had minimal effect; ACE2 affinity largely unaffected.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze structural basis for the observed ΔΔG changes.

evidence.quote_or_span: mutation at E484 in the Beta, Gamma, Kappa, and Omicron variant was identified as the critical mutation undermining the binding affinity
all mutations barely affected the affinity with ACE2

The Omicron variant combined the characteristics brought by mutation at E484 and N501, resulting in a decrease in affinity with Regdanvimab, P2B-2F6, Fab 2-15, and S2-M11 sensitive to E484 mutation, and S2-H14 sensitive to N501 and Y505 mutation.
the flexddG results revealed that mutations from SARS-CoV-2 variants diminished neutralizing antibody binding to varying degrees, which may be strongly correlated to their behavior in immune escape.
As L452R and T478K did not significantly impact the antibody interface $\Delta\Delta G$ like other mutations, we further inspected the mutant structure rendered by flexddG.
other three variants by the two unique mutations L452R and T478K located in the RBD region, which also impacted the variant's sensitivity to antibodies or vaccine-elicited sera.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: flexddG identified E484 as most disruptive, but structural mechanism unclear.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do specific mutations disrupt antibody-RBD interactions at the atomic level?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations disrupt polar contacts and hydrogen bonds at the interface.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Inspect mutant structures rendered by flexddG for P2B-2F6, S2-H14, and CB6.
evolution_trajectory.observation: E484 interacts with N33, Y34, R112 of P2B-2F6; mutation to K484 excludes R112 from polar network. For S2-H14, N501Y/Y505H synergistically diminish polar interactions. Omicron's E484A and N501Y/Y505H disrupt interactions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate computational predictions with in vitro binding assays.

evidence.quote_or_span: residue E484 of the RBD interacts with the N33 and Y34 of the light chain and R112 of the heavy chain of the P2B-2F6 antibody, and when the residue was mutated to K484, it excluded R112 from the polar interaction network and diminished the H-bond interaction with Y34.
for S2-H14, the N501Y/Y505H synergized the diminished polar interaction
Similar to E484K, E484A would exclude R112 of the heavy chain of P2B-2F6 from the polar interaction network, destabilizing the interface between RBD and antibody.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Computational predictions suggest E484 mutations most disruptive.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to experimentally measure binding affinity of variant spike proteins to antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variant spike proteins will show diminished binding affinity, especially those with E484 mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform BLI kinetic profiling of SARS-CoV-2 spike variants with neutralizing antibodies.

parameters.spike_variants: wild-type, Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, Kappa, Lambda, Omicron

evolution_trajectory.observation: All variant spike proteins manifested diminished constant binding affinity (KD). Omicron, Beta, Gamma, Kappa, and Lambda with E484 mutation displayed most resistance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze whether KD or maximal binding better indicates antigenicity alteration.

evidence.quote_or_span: It was shown that all SARS-CoV-2 variant spike proteins manifested diminished constant binding affinity to various antibodies.
Omicron, Beta, Gamma, Kappa, and Lambda variants with a mutation at E484 displayed the most potency in resistance to binding of nAbs

the constant binding affinity may not be an ideal indicator for binding status determination in a parallel study on SARS-CoV-2 spike protein variants. The normalized maximum binding capability may be better for surveillance of antigenicity alteration of the SARS-CoV-2 spike proteins.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BLI data shows diminished KD for variants, but some antibodies retain neutralization despite low KD.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Constant binding affinity (KD) may not fully capture antigenicity alteration; maximal binding might be more indicative.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Normalized maximal binding capability is a better indicator for surveillance of antigenicity alteration than KD.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare KD and maximal binding signals from BLI data for antibodies S2-H14, CB6, and Regdanvimab.
evolution_trajectory.observation: S2-H14 and CB6 showed little change in KD but significant reduction in maximal binding for N501 and K417 mutations. Regdanvimab partially preserved maximal binding to L452R despite dramatic KD decrease.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test neutralization to see if maximal binding predicts efficacy.

evidence.quote_or_span: the constant binding affinity may not be an ideal indicator for binding status determination in a parallel study on SARS-CoV-2 spike protein variants. The normalized maximum binding capability may be better for surveillance of antigenicity alteration of the SARS-CoV-2 spike proteins.
the maximal binding capability was identified as the critical factor for evaluating alterations of antibody binding

Although S2-H14 and CB6 displayed little change in $K_{D}$ value when confronted with the mutations at N501 and K417 during binding to the spike protein, their maximal binding capability was significantly reduced
Regdanvimab partially preserved its maximal binding to the spike protein with the L452R mutation but displayed a dramatic decrease of $K_{D}$

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Maximal binding identified as potentially better indicator than KD.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm immune escape and correlate with binding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pseudovirus neutralization will confirm immune escape, and neutralization efficacy will correlate with maximal binding rather than KD for certain antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform pseudovirus neutralization assay on variant spike-bearing pseudoviruses with antibody panel.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Omicron variant showed broad resistance; only S2-E12 and COV2-2196 could neutralize Omicron weakly. Neutralization efficacy highly correlated with binding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate computational predictions against neutralization results.

evidence.quote_or_span: we further evaluated the resulting immune escape effect in a pseudovirus neutralization assay
The Omicron variant spike was barely bound to almost all antibodies.
we further evaluated the resulting immune escape effect in a pseudovirus neutralization assay
We produced pseudoviruses bearing wild-type or mutant spike proteins harboring all mutations from the SARS-CoV-2 variants and determined the neutralizing $IC_{50}$ of the antibodies
Only S2-E12 and COV2-2196 could neutralize the infection of the Omicron variant in a somehow weakened manner

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Neutralization data available for comparison with computational predictions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How well do flexddG predictions match experimental neutralization?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Computational interface analysis can predict mutation impact on antibody efficacy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare flexddG ΔΔG values with neutralization IC50 shifts for key mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Computational interface analysis showed remarkable performance in predicting potential impact on antibody efficacy. Consistency between kinetic and immune escape characteristics highlighted that interruption of antibody-antigen interaction at atomic scale determined neutralization efficiency.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Revise understanding of binding determinants based on maximal binding findings.

evidence.quote_or_span: computational interface analysis showed remarkable performance in predicting the potential impact on antibody efficacy

The consistency of kinetic and immune escape characteristics of both the complete mutation set derived from the variants highlighted that interruption of the antibody-antigen interaction at the atomic scale, ascribed to mutations at single residues, determined the fate of antibody neutralization efficiency

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validation confirmed flexddG predictions, but some antibodies showed discordance between KD and neutralization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why do some antibodies with low KD still neutralize, while others with preserved KD fail?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Maximal binding, not KD, determines neutralization effectiveness for certain antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Re-evaluate the role of maximal binding in neutralization for S2-H14, CB6, and Regdanvimab.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Maximal binding was the determinant of effectiveness for S2-H14 and CB6. Regdanvimab retained neutralization against L452R despite low KD due to preserved maximal binding. A more cautious conclusion for antibody effectiveness should include maximal binding assessment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations of flexddG for non-interface mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: Maximal binding was the determinant of effectiveness for these antibodies
a more cautious conclusion for antibody effectiveness should be made based on the kinetic analysis, and a detailed assessment of maximal binding should be included in this consideration

For the delta variant, although Regdanvimab and P2B-2F6 were less affected by L452R than by E484K/A/Q according to the flexddG results, we found that their affinity for the delta spike protein variants was significantly decreased, indicating that other mutations beyond the RBD may influence antibody binding or that flexddG may underestimate its impact on antibody binding.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Revised understanding emphasizes maximal binding, but Omicron still shows unexpected broad resistance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: flexddG could not detect effects of Omicron mutations not directly at interface (G339, R346, S371, S373, S375) that may alter RBD conformation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Non-interface mutations may alter RBD conformation, affecting antibody binding indirectly.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitation that flexddG misses conformational effects of non-interface mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The vast majority of Omicron mutations displayed little influence on antibody binding as detected by flexddG. Mutations at G339, R346, S371, S373, S375 located on the side of RBD may not directly participate in interaction but could alter RBD conformation, which cannot be revealed by flexddG.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Propose antibody cocktails to overcome variant resistance.

evidence.quote_or_span: the vast majority of mutations from the Omicron variant displayed little influence on antibody binding as detected by flexddG

The mutations at G339, R346, S371, S373, and S375 located on the side of RBD may not directly participate in interaction with antibody but could alter the conformation of RBD through overall structural impact, which cannot be revealed by flexddG

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Single antibodies are vulnerable to escape mutations; flexddG can identify mutation vulnerabilities.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to overcome variant resistance given diverse mutation profiles?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Antibody cocktails targeting diverse epitopes could overcome variant resistance, guided by computational analysis.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose that appropriate combination of antibodies with different vulnerabilities could result in effective inhibition.
evolution_trajectory.observation: An appropriate combination of antibodies with different vulnerabilities to specific mutations could still result in effective inhibition of SARS-CoV-2 variant infection synergistic activity. This computational analysis offers a valuable reference for matching primary antibodies for such application scenarios.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude with study limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: an appropriate combination of antibodies with different vulnerabilities to specific mutations could still result in effective inhibition of SARS-CoV-2 variant infection synergistic activity
This computational analysis offers a valuable reference for matching primary antibodies for such application scenarios

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Study completed with main findings and proposed solutions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Study has inherent limitations that affect generalizability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Limitations include input structure resolution, in vitro vs. in vivo differences, limited antibody panel, and pseudovirus vs. authentic virus differences.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations to contextualize findings.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The unexpected low binding of Omicron variant spike to all antibodies may indicate that other mutations without clear signs on flexddG may participate in disruption. Data available under reasonable request.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: End of trajectory.

evidence.quote_or_span: the unexpected low binding of Omicron variant spike to all the antibodies may indicate that other mutations without clear signs on flexddG may participate in the disruption of RBD-antibody interaction.
Under reasonable request, the experimental data could be accessed through research data deposit (RDD) with RDD number RDDB2021176511.

success_verification.final_conclusion: Computational interface analysis (flexddG) effectively predicted mutation-driven immune escape, validated by in vitro assays. E484 mutations are most disruptive. Maximal binding, not KD, determines neutralization for some antibodies. Antibody cocktails targeting diverse epitopes may overcome variant resistance.
limitations: flexddG cannot detect effects of non-interface mutations that alter RBD conformation (G339, R346, S371, S373, S375)
Input structure resolution affects flexddG reliability
In vitro assays may not fully reflect in vivo
Limited antibody panel
Pseudovirus vs. authentic virus differences

source.title: Dual-expression system for blue fluorescent protein optimization

initial_request.research_problem: Blue fluorescent proteins (BFPs) are dimmer than other spectral counterparts and lack systematic characterization in mammalian cells and model organisms, limiting their use in multicolor imaging.
initial_request.known_context: BFPs are dimmer than other FPs; available BFPs not systematically characterized in mammalian cells and model organisms.
constraints: Must be monomeric
Must be bright in mammalian cells
Must be photostable
quantifiable_goals: Increase intracellular brightness in mammalian cells
Improve photostability
Achieve monomeric state

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BFPs are dimmer than other FPs and not systematically characterized in mammalian cells and model organisms.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: It is unclear whether high molecular brightness translates to high intracellular brightness in mammalian cells.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: High molecular brightness does not guarantee high intracellular brightness, necessitating mammalian cell screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a dual-expression vector to enable screening in both E. coli and mammalian cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: High molecular brightness does not always correspond to high intracellular brightness in cultured cells and in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need a vector that allows expression in both E. coli and mammalian cells for screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: blue FPs (BFPs) are characterized by lower brightness compared to other spectral counterparts
available BFPs were not systematically characterized for imaging in cultured mammalian cells and common model organisms
high molecular brightness does not always correspond to high intracellular brightness in cultured cells and in vivo

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validated pHybrid vector; mRuby3 chosen as parental FP.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate blue fluorescent variants from mRuby3.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Site-directed mutagenesis at chromophore-proximal positions will yield blue fluorescent clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform site-directed mutagenesis of mRuby3 at 9 positions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Site-directed libraries generated with degenerated codons at specified positions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Create larger libraries via random mutagenesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Site-directed libraries of mRuby3 were synthesized de novo as gBlocks (EpochLifescience) with degenerated codons at the following amino acid positions 67, 68, 71, 84, 147, 162, 176, 178, 201.
The gBlock was amplified with corresponding primers using PCR and subcloned into the pHybrid vector.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Site-directed libraries created.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need larger diversity for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Random mutagenesis will generate libraries of 10^6-10^7 clones with up to 15 mutations per 1000 bp.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform error-prone PCR on mRuby3.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Random libraries generated and electroporated into TOP10 E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen libraries using hierarchical approach.

evidence.quote_or_span: Random mutagenesis was performed with GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis Kits (Stratagene) under conditions enabling a mutation frequency of up to 15 mutations per 1000 base pairs.
The generated gene libraries in expression vectors were electroporated into TOP10 E. coli host cells (Biomed).
Typical random libraries for each screen with fluorescence-activated cell sorter (FACS) consisted of about $10^{6}-10^{7}$ independent clones.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Random libraries in E. coli.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify clones with high intracellular brightness in mammalian cells.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Hierarchical screening (FACS, colony picking, in vitro brightness, intracellular brightness) will identify top candidates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen ~10^7 clones via FACS, select colonies, measure in vitro and intracellular brightness.

parameters.screening_steps: FACS, colony selection, in vitro brightness, intracellular brightness in HEK cells

evolution_trajectory.observation: Library expressed in E. coli, FACS sorted, colonies selected, ~100 clones assessed in vitro and in HEK cells.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze correlation between in vitro and intracellular brightness.

evidence.quote_or_span: The library was expressed in E. coli and subjected to screening with a fluorescence activated cell sorter (FACS) followed by selection of the blue fluorescence colonies
About 100 selected clones were expressed in E. coli for in vitro measurements of spectral properties and brightness, and in HEK cells for assessment of intracellular brightness

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Screening data from first round.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Relationship between in vitro and intracellular brightness unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Low correlation between in vitro and intracellular brightness justifies mammalian cell screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze correlation and proceed with additional rounds of evolution.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Low correlation between in vitro brightness and intracellular brightness.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform two more rounds of directed evolution with hierarchical screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: high molecular brightness does not always correspond to high intracellular brightness in cultured cells and in vivo

evolution_trajectory.state_before: First round of evolution completed; low correlation observed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to further improve brightness and add photostability screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Additional rounds of directed evolution with hierarchical screening and photostability measurement will yield improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform two more rounds of directed evolution, adding photostability measurement in the final round.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Two more rounds performed; photostability measured in final round.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select best variants based on brightness × photostability product.

evidence.quote_or_span: we performed two more rounds of directed evolution as described above, selecting about 10 best clones from each round for further evolution
For the final round of evolution, we additionally measured intracellular photostability in HEK cells

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Final round of evolution completed; 7 best variants identified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select top variants for benchmarking.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants with highest product of normalized brightness and photostability will be the best candidates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select Electra1 and Electra2 based on >30% higher score.

parameters.metric: product of normalized brightness and photostability

evolution_trajectory.observation: Two variants (Electra1, Electra2) exhibited at least 30% higher score; common and unique mutations identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Benchmark Electras against existing BFPs in HEK cells.

evidence.quote_or_span: Two variants exhibited at least 30% higher score compared to the rest of the selected clones

Sequencing of the 7 variants revealed the following common mutations K10E/M67L/Y68F/R71K/E118G/K142E/N147F/C176A/F178I/H201Y/Q217L with the following unique mutations for the two top-performing variants D155N/G171D/N190D/I206V and K85E/P131H/N133K/Y236F
Blue-to-red fluorescence ratio in live HEK cells expressing 7 best performing blue mRuby3 variants using pHybrid-/mScarlet

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to benchmark Electras against existing BFPs in HEK cells for brightness, photostability, and monomeric state.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Electras will show improved intracellular brightness and photostability compared to mBlueberry2, EBFP2, mTagBFP2.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Benchmark Electra1, Electra2 against mBlueberry2, EBFP2, mTagBFP2 in HEK cells.

parameters.measurements: intracellular brightness, photostability, monomeric state (OSER assay)

evolution_trajectory.observation: Electras show high brightness and photostability; OSER assay indicates monomeric state but with high variability.

Normalized photobleaching curves for mBlueberry2, EBFP2, mTagBFP2, Electra1 and Electra2 expressed in HEK cells
MFI as a way to further validate true monomeric behavior
we observed high variability among experiments in MFI ratios and fraction of cells from previous studies

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Electras benchmarked in HEK cells.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test Electras as fusion tags for structural proteins.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Electras will perform well as fusion tags without disrupting localization.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test Electra1, Electra2, and mTagBFP2 fusions to structural proteins in HeLa cells.

parameters.fusions: COX8A, paxillin, β-actin, keratin, laminA, α-tubulin, H2B

evolution_trajectory.observation: Electras successfully label structural proteins; similar performance to mTagBFP2.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Benchmark in primary neurons.

evidence.quote_or_span: the next step was to evaluate the performance of Electras as protein fusion tags for structural proteins

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Electras validated as fusion tags.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test Electras in primary neurons.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Electras will show comparable brightness to mTagBFP2 and higher photostability than EBFP2 in neurons.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Benchmark Electras in primary hippocampal mouse neurons via rAAV transduction.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Electra1 and Electra2 brightness 1.28- and 1.1-fold lower than mTagBFP2, 1.12- and 1.3-fold higher than EBFP2; photostability: EBFP2 half-time 635±70 s, Electra1 185±10 s, Electra2 200±5 s, mTagBFP2 135±5 s.

evidence.quote_or_span: We continued the benchmarking of the selected BFPs in the cytosol of primary hippocampal mouse neurons through transduction with rAAV2/9 under human synapsin promoter.
28- and 1.1-fold lower than that of mTagBFP2, but 1.
12- and 1.3-fold higher than that of EBFP2 (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Electras tested in neurons.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to test Electras in vivo in C. elegans, zebrafish, and mice.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Electras will outperform mTagBFP2 in brightness in some organisms.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform in vivo imaging in C. elegans, zebrafish, and mice.
evolution_trajectory.observation: C. elegans: Electra1 and Electra2 2.3- and 2.1-fold brighter than mTagBFP2; zebrafish: Electra2 1.16- and 1.08-fold brighter; mice: Electra1 1.2-fold brighter; Electra1 forms puncta in neurons.

end-users should consider the tendency of Electra1 to form bright puncta upon expression in neurons in vivo.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: All validation data collected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Several limitations identified: photoactivation, OSER assay variability, in vivo brightness inconsistency.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: These limitations do not preclude use but require careful consideration.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Document limitations and conclude that Electras are useful BFPs with some caveats.

evidence.quote_or_span: We suggest that upon transient expression in HEK cells (36 h) when the protein is actively translated there is a larger fraction of the C-form compared to that under long-term expression in cultured neurons (7 days) and zebrafish larva (4–5 days), where photoactivation was not observed.
However, in contrast to analytical methods, the OSER assay may not be suitable for the absolute quantification of monomeric state.

validation_methods: Intracellular brightness measurement in HEK cells

source.title: Sample-efficient parameter exploration of the powder film drying process using experiment-based Bayesian optimization

initial_request.research_problem: Optimizing powder film drying process parameters is challenging due to multiscale, multiphysical phenomena and large parameter space (32,768 combinations). Traditional numerical models are impractical, and brute-force or empirical methods are inefficient.
initial_request.known_context: The process is controlled by many parameters and involves complex phenomena, making numerical models impractical and brute-force exploration infeasible.
constraints: Sample-efficient exploration required
Non-parametric method preferred
quantifiable_goals: Minimize film defects (defect ratio)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The powder film forming process is controlled by many parameters and involves complex phenomena, making numerical models impractical and brute-force exploration infeasible.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to efficiently explore the large parameter space to minimize defects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bayesian optimization (BO) is expected to be a suitable approach for production processes, enabling efficient parameter exploration with fewer experiments.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Adopt BO with Gaussian process regression (GPR) as surrogate model and upper confidence bound (UCB) as acquisition function.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BO is also expected to be a suitable approach for production processes, in which it is challenging to search for the optimal process parameters manually and heuristically.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to design experimental system and parameter space to apply BO.

evidence.quote_or_span: the powder film forming process is controlled by a number of parameters, and the phenomena entailed in the process are complex
BO is also expected to be a suitable approach for production processes, in which it is challenging to search for the optimal process parameters manually and heuristically

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BO hypothesis formulated; need experimental setup.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What experimental system and parameter space to use?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Drop casting with controlled drying temperature and time can be used to explore parameter space.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use drop casting with slurry containing carbon particles, Nafion, water, and IPA; control drying temperature over 5 time steps with 8 levels each.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The material slurry was dried while controlling the heating temperature, and the homogeneity of the resulting electrode film was quantitatively evaluated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need initial data to train surrogate model.

evidence.quote_or_span: we conducted experiments using drop casting, which can easily produce multiple samples with a small amount of slurry
The material slurry was dried while controlling the heating temperature, and the homogeneity of the resulting electrode film was quantitatively evaluated

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental system designed; need initial random experiments.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the baseline defect ratio distribution?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Random sampling will provide initial data for BO.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conduct 30 experiments with random temperature profiles.
evolution_trajectory.observation: For most of the temperature profiles, defect ratios of 30% to 50% were observed; however, in rare samples (3 out of 30 samples), the defect ratios were below 15%.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to evaluate defect ratio quantitatively for all samples.

evidence.quote_or_span: we conducted 30 experiments using random temperature profiles
For most of the temperature profiles, defect ratios of 30% to 50% were observed; however, in rare samples (3 out of 30 samples), the defect ratios were below 15% (Fig. 2a).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Initial random experiments completed; need defect evaluation method.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to quantitatively evaluate film defects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Image binarization can extract cracks and thin regions to compute defect ratio.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use image binarization to detect cracks and thin regions, compute defect ratio as proportion of defect area.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Cracks and exceedingly thin regions were extracted based on the brightness values of the surface images, and the proportion of the detected areas to the total area where the dropped ink spread was evaluated as the “defect ratio”.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to build surrogate model for BO.

evidence.quote_or_span: cracks and exceedingly thin regions were extracted based on the brightness values of the surface images, and the proportion of the detected areas to the total area where the dropped ink spread was evaluated as the “defect ratio”

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Defect ratio metric defined; initial data available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to predict defect ratio for untested parameters?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: GPR can model the defect ratio as a function of temperature profile.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build surrogate model using GPR and use UCB acquisition function to select next experiment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: To predict the defect ratio, we built a surrogate model using GPR. The acquisition function was set to the upper confidence bound (UCB).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to BO exploration phase.

evidence.quote_or_span: To predict the defect ratio, we built a surrogate model using GPR

an acquisition function was calculated for each temperature profile using the predicted values of the surrogate model, and the temperature profile with the highest score was adopted as the subsequent experimental
The acquisition function was set to the upper confidence bound (UCB) $^{50-52}$ . The UCB strategy balances exploration and exploitation during optimization.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BO loop initialized with initial data and surrogate model.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can BO find parameters with lower defect ratios?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BO exploration will identify temperature profiles with defect ratios below 10%.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run BO exploration phase with UCB acquisition.
evolution_trajectory.observation: In the exploration phase, the defect ratios were less than 10% for all the tested parameter sets, and the lowest value was 2.8%, which was obtained in experiments four and nine.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze common features of low-defect profiles.

evidence.quote_or_span: In the exploration phase, the defect ratios were less than 10% for all the tested parameter sets, and the lowest value was 2.8%, which was obtained in experiments four and nine.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BO exploration yielded low defect ratios.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What temperature profile pattern leads to low defects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Low defect ratios tend to occur with a low–high–low temperature profile.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze temperature profiles of low-defect experiments.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Low defect ratios tended to be observed in the temperature profiles wherein the temperature of the first step was low (30 °C), that of the second step was relatively high (above 60 °C), and that of the third step was also relatively low (below 60 °C).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Discuss drying mechanism.

evidence.quote_or_span: low defect ratios tended to be observed in the temperature profiles wherein the temperature of the first step was low (30 °C), that of the second step was relatively high (above 60 °C), and that of the third step was also relatively low (below 60 °C).

low defect ratios tended to be observed in the temperature profiles wherein the temperature of the first step was low (30 °C), that of the second step was relatively high (above 60 °C), and that of the third step was also relatively low (below 60 °C).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Low-defect profile identified; need to understand underlying mechanism.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why does the low–high–low temperature profile reduce defects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Low initial temperature prevents uneven particle distribution, skin formation reduces evaporation, and final heating relaxes stress.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Discuss drying mechanism based on observed profile.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Maintaining a low temperature caused the solvent to evaporate gently, leading to an increase in the solid phase ratio. Accelerated evaporation by increasing the temperature would not cause uneven precipitation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate BO efficiency.

evidence.quote_or_span: maintaining a low temperature caused the solvent to evaporate gently, leading to an increase in the solid phase ratio.
accelerated evaporation by increasing the temperature would not cause uneven precipitation.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Low-defect profile and mechanism discussed; need to quantify BO efficiency.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How efficient is BO compared to exhaustive search?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BO achieves 1000x efficiency over brute-force.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare number of experiments (40) to total combinations (32,768).
evolution_trajectory.observation: The total number of experiments conducted in this study was 40, corresponding to approximately 0.1% of all the possible parameter combinations. Therefore, the exploration was 1,000 times more efficient than evaluating all the parameters individually.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Discuss limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: The total number of experiments conducted in this study was 40, corresponding to approximately 0.1% of all the possible parameter combinations. Therefore, the exploration was 1,000 times more efficient than evaluating all the parameters individually.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BO validated as efficient; need to acknowledge limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the limitations of the approach?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Human interpretation of 5D space is impossible; parameter selection and evaluation criteria affect results.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitations: 5D space hard for humans, inappropriate parameter selection may increase cost, poor evaluation criteria can introduce measurement error.
evolution_trajectory.observation: It would be almost impossible for a human to understand the distribution of the predicted performance in a five-dimensional parameter space. Parameters that do not affect the process or are difficult to control may increase the cost of the experiments. If the evaluation criterion is not set appropriately, the experiments become more difficult and the measurement error may crucially affect the prediction results.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude study.

evidence.quote_or_span: it would be almost impossible for a human to understand the distribution of the predicted performance in a five-dimensional (in this study) parameter space.

parameters that do not affect the process or are difficult to control may increase the cost of the experiments. This contradicts the original purpose of a highly efficient search and makes the interpretation of the proposed parameter sets and the resulting maps difficult.
if the evaluation criterion is not set appropriately, the experiments become more difficult and the measurement error may crucially affect the prediction results.

validation_methods: Comparison of number of experiments to total parameter combinations
success_verification.final_conclusion: BO efficiently explored the parameter space, achieving defect ratios below 10% with a low–high–low temperature profile, using only 40 experiments out of 32,768 combinations.
limitations: Human cannot understand 5D parameter space
Inappropriate parameter selection may increase cost
Poor evaluation criteria can introduce measurement error

source.title: Photovoltaphores: pharmacophore models for identifying metal-free dyes for dye-sensitized solar cells

initial_request.research_problem: Develop pharmacophore models ('photovoltaphores') to identify metal-free dyes for dye-sensitized solar cells (DSSCs) via virtual screening, addressing the need for cost-effective, sustainable sensitizers.
initial_request.known_context: Pharmacophore models capture spatial arrangement of features shared by active compounds; DSSC dyes typically have D-π-A architecture.
constraints: Metal-free dyes only
Uniform experimental conditions (AM 1.5G, TiO2, iodide/triiodide)
quantifiable_goals: Identify virtual hits with appropriate HOMO/LUMO energies and UV-vis absorption
Validate enrichment over random selection

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No pharmacophore models exist for DSSC dye discovery.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can standard pharmacophoric features capture key properties of active DSSC dyes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pharmacophore models using standard features (AR, HI, HBA, NIC) can identify metal-free dyes for DSSCs by capturing the D-π-A architecture.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Proceed to build and validate pharmacophore models.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated based on known D-π-A architecture and pharmacophoric features.

evidence.quote_or_span: The ZINC15 in-stock library comprised of more than 13.8 M compounds was screened against all 18 high activity-based pharmacophore models.
we have adopted a consensus approach focusing on compounds that matched at least nine models. 5636 compounds met this criterion.
