source.title: Engineering Bacillus pumilus alkaline serine protease to increase its low-temperature proteolytic activity by directed evolution

initial_request.research_problem: The mesophilic alkaline serine protease DHAP from Bacillus pumilus BA06 has high catalytic efficiency at ~50 °C but low activity at low temperatures, limiting its industrial use.
initial_request.target_object: DHAP (alkaline serine protease from Bacillus pumilus BA06)
initial_request.known_context: DHAP is a mesophilic enzyme with optimal activity around 50 °C. Previous site-directed mutagenesis efforts have been made to improve cold activity.
constraints: Maintain or improve thermostability while enhancing cold activity.
quantifiable_goals: Increase proteolytic activity at low temperature (15 °C) compared to wild-type.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DHAP has low cold activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can directed evolution improve the cold activity of DHAP?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Directed evolution can improve the cold activity of DHAP.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Proceed with directed evolution approach.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Directed evolution is a viable strategy to enhance cold activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Construct a mutant library to test the hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Here we attempted to improve the cold activity of DHAP via directed evolution.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No mutant library exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate genetic diversity for screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR with dITP and DNA shuffling will create a diverse mutant library.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct a random mutant library using error-prone PCR and DNA shuffling.

tool_or_method.name: Error-prone PCR with dITP and DNA shuffling

evolution_trajectory.observation: More than 100,000 independent transformants were obtained.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen the library for improved cold activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: Finally, more than 100,000 independent transformants were harvested from the LB agar plates and used to isolate the plasmid DNAs, which were stored at -20 °C as the mutation library.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Library of >100,000 clones available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which clones have improved cold activity and retained thermostability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A two-round screening process will identify variants with enhanced cold activity and maintained thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen the library using milk plate halo size, then 96-well plate assays at 50 °C and 15 °C, plus thermostability test at 60 °C.

tool_or_method.name: Two-round screening: milk plate halo, 96-well plate assays, thermostability test

evolution_trajectory.observation: The screening process was performed; 36 final clones were selected for further analysis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sequence the library to assess mutation frequency.

evidence.quote_or_span: another set of the supernatant samples was pre-treated at 60 °C for 15 min and the survival activity was measured at 50 °C
The caseinolytic activity was primarily assayed at both 15 °C and 50 °C for the 36 variants and the wt of DHAP.
Finally, more than 100,000 independent transformants were harvested from the LB agar plates and used to isolate the plasmid DNAs, which were stored at -20 °C as the mutation library.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Library screened, but mutation quality unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the mutation frequency and spectrum in the library?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Sequencing random clones will reveal mutation frequency and bias.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence 21 random clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mutation frequency and spectrum were evaluated from 21 clones.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Identify specific variants with improved activity from the 36 final clones.

evidence.quote_or_span: To evaluate the mutation frequency, 21 independent transformants were randomly selected for DNA sequencing.
The caseinolytic activity was primarily assayed at both 15 °C and 50 °C for the 36 variants and the wt of DHAP.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 36 final clones selected from screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which specific variants have increased cold activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some of the 36 clones will show increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize the 36 clones and identify variants with increased activity at 15 °C.

tool_or_method.name: Proteolytic activity assay with casein and synthetic peptide substrates

evolution_trajectory.observation: Seven variants (P9S, A1G/K27Q, A38V, A116T, T162I, S182R, T243S) showed increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C towards both substrates.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate thermostability of these seven variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Seven variants (P9S, A1G/K27Q, A38V, A116T, T162I, S182R, and T243S) of DHAP from B. pumilus were obtained via directed evolution.
all of the variants had increased proteolytic activity at 15 °C towards both the casein and synthetic peptide substrates.
Subsequently, seven variants were selected for further assays.
The caseinolytic activity was primarily assayed at both 15 °C and 50 °C for the 36 variants and the wt of DHAP.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Seven variants identified with increased cold activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these variants retain thermostability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Most variants will retain thermostability comparable to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess thermostability of the seven variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Except T243S, the thermostability of the variants did not decrease compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Quantify activity improvements of the seven variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: With the exception of variant T243S, the thermostability of these variants did not decrease in comparison with the wild-type enzyme.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Seven variants with increased cold activity and mostly retained thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the quantitative improvements in caseinolytic activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants will show enhanced caseinolytic activity at various temperatures, with some showing high fold-increases at 15 °C.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure caseinolytic activity of the seven variants at different temperatures.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All seven variants showed enhanced caseinolytic activity. A1G/K27Q and T162I had 2.62- and 2.41-fold increases at 15 °C, respectively.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Combine beneficial mutations to further improve activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: Figure 1 shows that all of the seven variants achieved enhanced caseinolytic activity at different temperatures compared with the wt.
the caseinolytic activity of two variants (A1G/K27Q and T162I) was increased by 2.62- and 2.41-fold at 15 °C, respectively, compared with the wt.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Single variants show up to 2.62-fold improvement.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can combining beneficial mutations yield additive or synergistic effects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Combining P9S, K27Q, and T162I will further increase cold activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct four new variants by combining P9S, K27Q, and T162I via site-directed mutagenesis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Four combined variants constructed.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test combined variants for caseinolytic activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: Based on the above results, four new variants were constructed by the combination of three amino acid substitutions: P9S, K27Q, and T162I.
the caseinolytic activity of two variants (A1G/K27Q and T162I) was increased by 2.62- and 2.41-fold at 15 °C, respectively, compared with the wt.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Combined variants constructed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do combined variants show further improved activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Combined variants will exhibit higher caseinolytic activity than single variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure caseinolytic activity of combined variants at 15 °C and 50 °C.
evolution_trajectory.observation: P9S/K27Q, P9S/T162I, and P9S/K27Q/T162I showed ~5-fold increase at 15 °C and 2.8-fold at 50 °C compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Evaluate thermostability of combined variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: the caseinolytic activity of the mutant (P9S/K27Q, P9S/T162I, P9S/K27Q/T162I) was increased by about 5-fold and 2.8-fold compared with the wt at 15 °C and 50 °C, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Combined variants show high activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do combined variants maintain thermostability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some combined variants may have altered thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess thermostability of combined variants and compare with single variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: T243S and K27Q/T162I showed improved thermostability; S182R and P9S/K27Q/T162I showed reduced thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform structural analysis to interpret results.

evidence.quote_or_span: The slope of two variants (T243S and K27Q/T162I) was slower than that of the wt, indicating that their thermostability was improved.
the thermostability of two variants (S182R and P9S/K27Q/T162I) was reduced compared with the wt.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental data on activity and thermostability collected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What structural basis explains the observed effects?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology modeling will reveal how substitutions affect flexibility, electrostatics, and stability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform homology modeling of DHAP and analyze substitution sites.
evolution_trajectory.observation: P9S may increase flexibility without thermostability loss; loop substitutions (A38, T162, S182) may increase kcat but can decrease thermostability; K27Q may change electrostatic potential and increase cold activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude on integration of cold activity and thermostability.

evidence.quote_or_span: the four substitution sites (P9, A38, T162, and S182) were predicated to be exposed on the protein surface.
substitution of P9 with S9 may lead to more flexibility in this region of the protein (Fig. 7a)
substitution of P9 with S9 in DHAP did not result in loss of thermostability (Fig. 4b).
These loop regions are thought to confer flexibility in any given protein.
The combined variants of P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I exhibited an approximate 5-fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C and almost no loss of thermostability.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Combined variants P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I show ~5-fold increase at 15 °C with no thermostability loss.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can cold activity and thermostability be integrated into a single variant?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Enhancement of cold activity and thermostability can be integrated into a single variant.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I achieve the goal.
evolution_trajectory.observation: P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I achieved ~5-fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C without thermostability loss. Effects of individual substitutions are accumulative.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Final conclusion.

evidence.quote_or_span: four combined variants were constructed from three sites: P9, K27, and T162, and catalytic activity with the macromolecular substrate casein was found to be further increased, especially at low temperature.
the variant (P9S/K27Q) showed an increase in caseinolytic activity of more than 5-fold compared with the wt
the effects of individual substitutions on catalytic activity may be accumulative and can be integrated into a single variant
the enhancement of cold activity and thermostability can be integrated into a single variant of bacterial alkaline protease.
The combined variants of P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I exhibited an approximate 5-fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C and almost no loss of thermostability.

metrics: Fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C
success_verification.final_conclusion: Directed evolution successfully improved the cold activity of DHAP. Combined variants P9S/K27Q and P9S/T162I achieved ~5-fold increase in caseinolytic activity at 15 °C without loss of thermostability, demonstrating that cold activity and thermostability can be integrated.
limitations: Some variants (e.g., T243S, S182R, P9S/K27Q/T162I) showed reduced thermostability.
The study focused on caseinolytic activity; performance on other substrates may vary.
Structural interpretations are based on homology modeling, not experimental structures.

source.title: Engineering a vitamin B12 high-throughput screening system by riboswitch sensor in Sinorhizobium meliloti

initial_request.research_problem: Low vitamin B12 yield in Sinorhizobium meliloti despite gene overexpression; need for a high-throughput screening method to identify hyperproducing mutants from random mutagenesis libraries.
initial_request.target_object: Vitamin B12 production in Sinorhizobium meliloti
initial_request.known_context: Previous efforts overexpressed VB12 pathway genes but yield remained low; riboswitches can act as metabolite-responsive genetic sensors.
constraints: Need for high-throughput screening compatible with FACS
Sensor must respond specifically to intracellular VB12 levels
input_data: Engineered strains Sm320, Sm320-N8, Sm320-L6 with different VB12 production capacities
quantifiable_goals: Identify a riboswitch that responds to VB12 concentration
Develop a FACS-based screening system
Isolate mutants with >10% increase in VB12 yield

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No high-throughput screening method for VB12 in S. meliloti; existing strains have low yield.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which riboswitch can function as a VB12 sensor in S. meliloti?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A riboswitch from a known VB12-responsive element can be coupled to GFP to report intracellular VB12 levels.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen eight candidate riboswitches in three engineered strains with different VB12 production capacities.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Five riboswitches showed no differential response; three (including SY-btuB) showed VB12-dependent repression.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: SY-btuB showed the most sensitive response but acts as a repressor (high VB12 → low GFP), requiring signal inversion.

evidence.quote_or_span: Eight riboswitches were selected based on the sequence reported by Vitreschak et al.
The plasmids were then separately transferred into Sm320, Sm320-N8 and Sm320-L6 (engineered mutants with different production capacities)

The strains containing the plasmids were grown at 30 °C for 12 h and were used to provide shake flask fermentation for 3 days with 10% (v/v) inoculant and then tested for GFP expression by flow cytometry
The mutant MC5–2 showed $VB_{12}$ production of $156 \pm 4.2$ mg/L, which was 21.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Three riboswitches showed VB12-dependent repression; SY-btuB is a candidate.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which riboswitch is the most sensitive and suitable for a screening system?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SY-btuB from Salmonella typhimurium is the best regulatory device due to its sensitivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select SY-btuB as the sensor element.
evolution_trajectory.observation: SY-btuB was identified as the most sensitive VB12-responsive riboswitch, but it acts as a repressor (high VB12 → low GFP).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to invert the signal so that high VB12 yields high fluorescence for FACS enrichment.

evidence.quote_or_span: the btuB element from Salmonella typhimurium was identified as the best regulatory device

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SY-btuB represses GFP when VB12 is high.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to convert the repressive response into a positive fluorescence signal for FACS?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A dual-repression system can invert the signal: VB12 represses a repressor of GFP, so high VB12 leads to high GFP.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a genetic circuit where SY-btuB controls a repressor of GFP.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Dual-repression system designed to yield high GFP when VB12 is high.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate the sensor by sorting a mixed population of strains with known VB12 levels.

evidence.quote_or_span: To identify a sensitive element that allows efficient screening of positive mutants with high $VB_{12}$ production, eight different potential riboswitches (Table 2) were selected according to the criteria described above, plasmids containing $VB_{12}$ -dependent riboswitches coupled to a GFP reporter were separately transformed into Sm320, Sm320-L6 and Sm320-N8, then the expression levels of GFP under the control of the eight riboswitches were compared.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Dual-repression sensor constructed but not tested with mixed populations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the sensor enrich high-producing cells from a mixture?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS sorting of the top 1% fluorescent cells will enrich the high-producing Sm320 strain.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Mix Sm320, Sm320-L6, Sm320-N8, apply sensor, sort top 1% GFP, and identify sorted cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 84% of sorted cells were the high-producing Sm320 strain.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sensor validated; proceed to mutagenesis and screening of a mutant library.

evidence.quote_or_span: nearly 84% of sorted cells were identified as the high-producing Sm320 strain (Fig. 5c).
As expected, nearly 84% of sorted cells were identified as the high-producing Sm320 strain (Fig.

To identify a sensitive element that allows efficient screening of positive mutants with high $VB_{12}$ production, eight different potential riboswitches (Table 2) were selected according to the criteria described above, plasmids containing $VB_{12}$ -dependent riboswitches coupled to a GFP reporter were separately transformed into Sm320, Sm320-L6 and Sm320-N8, then the expression levels of GFP under the control of the eight riboswitches were compared.
The plasmids were then separately transferred into Sm320, Sm320-N8 and Sm320-L6 (engineered mutants with different production capacities)

Genome-scale gene expression analysis of Sm320 and Sm320MC5–2 revealed that the mutant genes were located in the $VB_{12}$ metabolic pathway, heme branch pathway, ABC transporters, $VB_{12}$ precursor synthesis pathway, tricarboxylic acid cycle and other metabolic pathways.
sources “2% CSL” by “2% AYP, 1% peptone” in the fermentation medium when the strains were screened by FACS, and the comparison was shown in Additional file 1: Figure S5.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sensor validated; need to generate genetic diversity for screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What ARTP mutagenesis conditions yield optimal mutation frequency?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: 60 s ARTP exposure will give 80-85% lethality, suitable for generating a diverse mutant library.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Optimize ARTP treatment time by measuring lethality; use 60 s for library generation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 60 s exposure gave 80-85% lethality; mutant library of ~6×10^7 mutants generated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen the mutant library using the validated FACS sensor.

evidence.quote_or_span: To determine the optimal treatment time, S. meliloti was cultured for 72 h (mid-log phase) and then subjected to ARTP treatment
Lethality rate was calculated for determining the appropriate treatment time

source.title: Metabolic engineering of Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 for the production of butadiene precursor

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 to produce crotyl diphosphate, a butadiene precursor, from crotonyl-CoA via a three-enzyme pathway (ADHE2, THK, IPK).
initial_request.known_context: No known enzymes for converting crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply an in vitro one-pot strategy to identify promiscuous enzymes contributing to butadiene synthesis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strategy designed to efficiently identify promiscuous enzymes for butadiene synthesis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to test candidate kinases for crotonol phosphorylation.

evidence.quote_or_span: One of the major challenges in this work is the lack of reports on enzymes catalyzing the biochemical reactions from crotonyl-CoA to butadiene.
Herein, an in vitro one-pot strategy was applied for sequentially converting crotonol into crotyl diphosphate.
this strategy is able to efficiently identify promiscuous enzymes contributing to butadiene synthesis

evolution_trajectory.observation: THK produced the highest crotyl monophosphate among candidates; background activity from native glycerate kinase detected (0.06 U/mg using 0.5 mM crotonol).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize THK kinetics to assess its efficiency.

evidence.quote_or_span: THK was the most suitable kinase among three candidates for phosphorylating crotonol to crotyl monophosphate in M. extorquens AM1.

glycerate kinase in M. extorquens AM1 was able to phosphorylate crotonol with an activity of 0.06 U/mg using 0.5 mM crotonol as substrate

source.title: Protein-altering and regulatory genetic variants near GATA4 implicated in bicuspid aortic valve

initial_request.research_problem: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations.
initial_request.known_context: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; only one previous GWAS (n=68) with no significant findings.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; previous GWAS underpowered (n=68) with no significant findings.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of robust genetic associations for BAV.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A larger GWAS enriched for protein-altering variants will identify genetic variants associated with BAV.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a GWAS with 466 cases and 4,660 controls, replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls, using Illumina Human CoreExome array.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Study design established.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform GWAS to discover associated variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: Despite the prevalence, importance and heritability of BAV, its genetic origins remain elusive.
Only one previous genome-wide association study (GWAS) for BAV has been published in a limited number of cases (n=68; ref. 14), which did not identify any genome-wide significant results.
here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform GWAS discovery analysis.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Replicate findings in independent cohorts.

evidence.quote_or_span: Ser377Gly (rs3729856) in GATA4, which encodes a cardiac-specific transcription factor that is 151 kilobases(kb) away from the first variant ( $P_{after-replication}=8.8\times10^{-8}$ ).

here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.
Ser377Gly) was associated with an increased risk of ToF, whereas the low-frequency noncoding variant (rs6601627) was not associated.
among the noncoding variant rs6601627 and its 115 correlated variants, 108 variants have MAF<0.01 and 73 are monomorphic in East Asians

source.title: Next-generation sequencing enables the discovery of more diverse positive clones from a phage-displayed antibody library

initial_request.research_problem: Conventional phage display screening yields limited diversity of positive clones due to uncontrolled enrichment.
initial_request.known_context: Phage display libraries can contain up to 10^11 variants, but bio-panning enrichment cannot be controlled, frequently resulting in a limited number of clones.
constraints: NGS read length limits full scFv sequencing
Two-step linker PCR is low-throughput
input_data: Three chicken scFv phage-displayed libraries immunized with PSA
Phagemid DNA from rounds 0-4 of bio-panning
quantifiable_goals: Identify more diverse positive clones than conventional colony screening (22 unique HCDR3 sequences)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Conventional phage display screening yields limited clone diversity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether NGS can identify a greater number of clones than conventional methods.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: NGS can identify more clones than conventional methods.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply NGS to screen clones from a library and compare with conventional screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated based on known limitation of phage display.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design libraries and panning to test the hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: a major drawback of phage display technology is that because the degree of the enrichment cannot be controlled during the bio-panning process, it frequently results in a limited number of clones
we applied next-generation sequencing (NGS) to screen clones from a library and determine whether a greater number of clones can be identified using NGS than using conventional methods

evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct three chicken scFv libraries and perform four rounds of bio-panning on PSA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Three libraries constructed and panned.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform conventional colony screening to establish baseline diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: Three white leghorn chickens were immunized and boosted four times with recombinant human PSA
three phage-displayed libraries of chicken scFvs were constructed using the pComb3XSS phagemid vector
Four rounds of bio-panning were performed to screen scFv clones from the library

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Libraries constructed and panned.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Low diversity motivates NGS analysis to explore deeper diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: A total of 300 clones (100 clones in each library) exhibiting an optical density of >0.3 at 405 nm were considered to be positive, and their scFv gene sequence was determined by Sanger sequencing analysis.
A total of 300 clones (100 clones in each library) exhibiting an optical density of >0.

All 22 HCDR3 sequences in clones found via the conventional colony screening method existed among the HCDR3 sequences obtained from NGS analysis of phagemid DNA prepared after the fourth round of bio-panning (Table 1).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Full diversity of HCDR3 sequences across panning rounds.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform NGS on phagemid DNA from rounds 0-4 using MiSeq.
evolution_trajectory.observation: NGS data obtained; 44-53% reads retained after alignment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Cluster HCDR3 sequences by enrichment patterns.

evidence.quote_or_span: A total of 15 sets of phagemid DNA including three initial chicken scFv libraries and three libraries obtained after each of four rounds of bio-panning were analyzed using a MiSeq system
A total of 15 sets of phagemid DNA (three chicken libraries from bio-panning rounds 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4) were used for NGS analysis.
44–53% of the original sequences were retained after aligning with OmpSeq primer sequence

All 22 HCDR3 sequences in clones found via the conventional colony screening method existed among the HCDR3 sequences obtained from NGS analysis of phagemid DNA prepared after the fourth round of bio-panning (Table 1).
Two out of the 22 unique HCDR3 sequences belonged to cluster 1, and the other 20 HCDR3 sequences belonged to clusters 2, 3 or 4.

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Visualize enrichment patterns with heatmaps.

evidence.quote_or_span: An optimized number of clusters in the merged sequence read counts was estimated using the clValid algorithm
the assessment for each and every clustering is represented in the Dunn index
Unsupervised hierarchical clustering analysis was used to cluster HCDR3 sequences according to the number of clusters estimated by clValid
clValid predicted that 2–6 clusters would be the most dependable in the HCDR3 sequence count profile data (Table 3).

evidence.quote_or_span: HCDR3 sequences in cluster 1 were highly abundant before bio-panning and up to the second bio-panning round. However, there was a sudden impoverishment in rounds 3 and 4 of bio-panning.

In contrast, HCDR3 sequences that belonged to clusters 2 and 3 (including cluster 4 of library 1) showed the opposite pattern. Their populations were very low before bio-panning, remained low after the second round of bio-panning, and started to enrich from the third round of bio-panning.

evidence.quote_or_span: To rebuild real scFv clones from the virtual HCDR3 sequences in the clusters, we performed two-step linker PCR
For each cluster, 1–5 HCDR3 sequences newly identified from the fourth round of bio-panning via NGS analysis were selected arbitrarily (Table 4).
These selected sequences were used to synthesize the primers to retrieve the whole scFv gene from the phagemid DNA.
The scFv gene was prepared in two-step linker PCR using the primers and cloned into a phagemid vector (Figure 3).
Of the 40 clones retrieved from the phagemid DNA pool prepared after the fourth round of bio-panning, positive binding reactivity was confirmed in 26 clones.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Binding reactivity of clones from different clusters.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform phage ELISA on retrieved clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 85.7% of cluster 1 clones non-binding; 80.8% of clusters 2-4 clones binding.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze why some high-proportion clones were missed by conventional screening.

when a certain HCDR3 sequence was enriched through the bio-panning process, the clone with the HCDR3 is more likely to be positive; in this study over 80% (21 out of 26 clones)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Binding data obtained.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Examine proportions of missed clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Some clones with >1% proportion (e.g., 2.16%, 4.79%) not found by colony screening.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations of NGS and retrieval method.

evidence.quote_or_span: Why these clones with such high proportions were not identified in the conventional colony screening method is not clear; however, it might be caused by either inherent toxicity with phage assembly or interference from bacterial growth.
The proportion of two positive clones in library 1 and library 3 were 2.16 and 4.
Four clones in library 1 and one clone in library 3 exhibited a proportion of $>1\%$ among the HCDR3 sequences obtained after the fourth round.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: NGS and retrieval method demonstrated.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Limitations of NGS and two-step linker PCR.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitations for future improvement.

evidence.quote_or_span: sequencing of the entire single-chain variable fragment (scFv) gene, which contains 750–800 bases, could not be achieved using any of these NGS platforms
Using currently available NGS tools, it is not possible to sequence the entire scFv gene, which is about 750 bp in length, without error.
However, there is always the possibility of cross-priming between clones during the PCR process.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: All results and limitations considered.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Overall effectiveness of NGS + two-step linker PCR.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: NGS analysis of HCDR3 and two-step linker PCR effectively retrieve antigen-specific scFv clones missed by conventional screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate method as effective for discovering diverse binders.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Method successfully identified additional diverse positive clones.

evidence.quote_or_span: NGS analysis of the HCDR3 sequence, and two-step linker PCR using PCR primers based on this sequence, provide an effective way to retrieve antigen-specific scFv clones that are difficult to identify by the conventional colony screening method.
Enrichment of the HCDR3 sequence over the bio-panning process is a positive predictive factor in the selection of scFv clones harboring binding reactivity.

source.title: Pharmacophore anchor models of flaviviral NS3 proteases lead to drug repurposing for DENV infection

initial_request.research_problem: Flaviviral drug discovery suffers from low hit rates, strain-specific efficacy, and resistance due to failure to capture key pharmacological features of the large, shallow NS3 protease active site.
initial_request.known_context: Virtual screening methods often fail to capture key pharmacological features of the target active site critical for protein function inhibition.

Screen FDA drugs to find active compounds against DENV NS3 protease

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Flaviviral drug discovery suffers from low hit rate, strain-specific efficacy, and resistance due to failure to capture key pharmacological features of NS3 protease active site.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Existing virtual screening methods fail to capture key pharmacological features of the target active site.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A pharmacophore anchor-based strategy using site-moiety maps can capture key interactions for drug design and discovery of flaviviral NS3 proteases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose pharmacophore anchor-based strategy.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to build pharmacophore anchor models from docking data.

evidence.quote_or_span: Most of the virtual screening methods in flaviviral drug discovery suffer from a low hit rate, strain-specific efficacy differences, and susceptibility to resistance. It is because they often fail to capture the key pharmacological features of the target active site critical for protein function inhibition.
we proposed the use of pharmacophore anchor based strategy (using site-moiety map [28]) for drug design and discovery of the flaviviral NS3 proteases.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis proposed but no models built.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to construct pharmacophore anchor models from interaction data.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Docking a large compound library and analyzing interaction profiles will reveal conserved pharmacophore anchors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Dock 187,740 compounds into four NS3 proteases, select top 3000 poses, and analyze interaction profiles to build PA models.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Docking performed and top 3000 poses selected for each protease.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Align PA models to identify conserved core anchors.

evidence.quote_or_span: we docked a 187,740 compound library into the extracted active sites (Methods: Proteins-compound datasets) of four NS3 proteases of HCV, DENV, WNV and JEV (Fig. 1a) using an in-house docking tool GEMDOCK
To build the PA/CPA model we collected a large compound library of 187,740 compounds, composed of non-redundant compounds of maybridge and natural product datasets from ZINCDB [41].
For each protease, the top 3000 compound poses ( $\sim$ 0.015%) based on binding energies were selected.
For each of the four proteases, top-ranked 3000 hits (based on best calculated interaction energy) are selected to construct interaction profiles.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: PA models built for each protease.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify conserved anchors across flaviviral NS3 proteases.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Aligning PA models will reveal common core anchors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Align four PA models to identify conserved core anchors.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Five common core anchors (CEH1, CH3, CH7, CV1, CV3) discovered, forming the CPA model.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate the CPA model with known inhibitors.

evidence.quote_or_span: When we aligned these four PA models as in Fig. 2a, we discovered five common core anchors (pink outline) and some specific anchors.
The core anchors along with the aligned protease active sites formed the Core Pharmacophore Anchor (CPA) model (Fig. 2b).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: CPA model built.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that anchors are biologically relevant and explain inhibitor binding.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Conserved anchor residues should be critical for activity, and known inhibitors should occupy these anchors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate PA/CPA models by conservation/mutation analysis and by analyzing binding of 89 known NS3 protease inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Models explained activities of most inhibitors; CEH1 and CH3 anchors critical.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply validated models to screen FDA drugs against DENV.

evidence.quote_or_span: For validating our PA/CPA models, we examined conservation and mutation-activity for anchor residues and explored the binding mechanisms of 89 known NS3 protease inhibitors (Fig. 1d).
For validating our PA/CPA models, we examined conservation and mutation-activity for anchor residues and explored the binding mechanisms of 89 known NS3 protease inhibitors (Fig.

We primarily evaluated our PA/CPA models and anchors by analyzing the evolutionary conservation of anchor residues and effect of their mutation on the protease enzymatic activity (Additional file 1: Note 3, Figure S3 and Table S1).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validated CPA model for DENV NS3 protease.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify potential repurposable drugs targeting DENV NS3 protease.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Integrated anchor-based virtual screening of FDA drugs will yield active compounds.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply integrated anchor-based virtual screening to DENV NS3 protease using 1384 FDA drugs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Top 1000 by energy, then top 100 by anchor occupancy, then top 10 by substrate similarity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select candidates for experimental testing.

evidence.quote_or_span: we applied this integrated approach to the target case of DENV NS3 protease for screening hits from the 1384 FDA drug dataset.

The compounds were first docked into DENV NS3 protease active site (using GEMDOCK virtual screening protocol: 10 poses per compound), followed by calculation of interaction energies and occupancy of anchors
We first selected the top 1000 compounds based on best calculated GEMDOCK interaction energies. From them, the top 100 compound poses with highest anchor occupancy scores were chosen
the binding poses and anchor occupancy patterns of these top 100 compounds were compared to that of dengue NS3 protease substrate-mimic ligand Bz-Nle-KKR-H from the PDB ID: 3U1I

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Top 10 candidates identified from virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to select most promising candidates for experimental testing.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Compounds with low interaction energies, high anchor occupancy, and substrate-like poses will be active.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select boceprevir, telaprevir, and asunaprevir based on docking energy, anchor occupancy, and similarity to substrate.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Three -previr drugs chosen for further analysis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test candidates in vitro.

evidence.quote_or_span: We chose three -previr drugs boceprevir, telaprevir and asunaprevir for further analysis

The binding models of the three candidates in the DENV NS3 protease were obtained by selecting the docking poses of the -previr compounds with low calculated interaction energies, adequate occupation of DENV PA model anchors and similar conformation and interactions to that of the ligand in 3U1I.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Candidates selected computationally.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to determine antiviral activity and cytotoxicity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Telaprevir and asunaprevir will inhibit DENV replication, while boceprevir may not due to missing CEH1 anchor.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test candidates in MTT cytotoxicity assay and DENV plaque formation assay on BHK cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Boceprevir inactive; telaprevir and asunaprevir active with EC50 values determined.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze structure-anchor-activity relationships to explain results.

evidence.quote_or_span: In MTT assay for cytotoxicity, Huh7 cells were plated in a 96-well plate and incubated overnight for cell attachment. Later, the cells were treated with different concentrations of candidate compounds and incubated for 24 h.
Cell culture supernatants were obtained from DENV2-NGC virus-infected (m.o.i: 0.5) Huh-7 cells with drug treatment and without drug treatment (only DMSO).
The collected supernatants were serially diluted ( $10^{-1}$ , $10^{-2}$ to $10^{-6}$ -fold dilution) and were added to BHK cell monolayer.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental EC50 values obtained.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to explain why boceprevir is inactive and why asunaprevir is more potent than telaprevir.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CEH1 anchor engagement is critical; boceprevir leaves CEH1 empty; asunaprevir occupies additional anchors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform SAAR studies using PA/CPA models to explain binding mechanisms and activities.
evolution_trajectory.observation: CEH1 anchor engagement critical; boceprevir leaves CEH1 empty; asunaprevir occupies DH9 and DV9 anchors enhancing potency.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Explain differential potencies between DENV and HCV.

evidence.quote_or_span: The structure-anchor-activity relationships (SAAR) showed that our PA/CPA model anchors explained the observed in-vitro activities of the candidates.
we observed that the CEH1 anchor engagement was critical for the activities of telaprevir and asunaprevir while the extent of inhibitor anchor occupation guided their efficacies.
For boceprevir, we noticed that the -CO-CONH2 group (blue-colored) occupied the DH2 anchor at S1 subsite, while CEH1 anchor at S1' near the oxyanion hole is left empty.
we pursued Structure-Anchor-Activity Relationship (SAAR) studies by employing our PA/CPA models and anchors to explain binding mechanisms and activities of boceprevir, telaprevir and asunaprevir

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SAAR explained DENV activities.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why are telaprevir and asunaprevir less potent against DENV than HCV?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Differential anchor occupancies in DENV vs HCV explain lower µM potencies.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare anchor occupancies of telaprevir and asunaprevir in DENV and HCV NS3 proteases.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Differential anchor occupancies explain lower potencies against DENV compared to HCV.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations of the study.

evidence.quote_or_span: The differential anchor occupancies of asunaprevir and telaprevir in DENV and HCV NS3 protease active sites are the reason for their differential efficacies

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Study completed with identified hits.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Docking inaccuracies for some inhibitors led to incorrect anchor occupancy predictions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Improved docking methods may yield more accurate anchor occupancy predictions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitation: occasional docking inaccuracies for some inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.observation: For few inhibitors, anchor occupancies could not exactly explain binding and activity, likely due to incorrect docking poses.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note dataset size limitation.

evidence.quote_or_span: for few inhibitors, occasionally the anchor occupancies could not exactly explain the mechanisms of binding and activity
the predicted activity according to docking pose-derived anchor occupancy is erroneous most likely due to the incorrect binding poses of these inhibitors predicted by docking

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Docking inaccuracies noted.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: FDA drug dataset too small to find novel nanomolar inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Larger libraries may yield better inhibitors.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitation: small FDA dataset (1384 compounds).
evolution_trajectory.observation: The 1384 FDA drug dataset was too small to find novel nanomolar inhibitors; further screening of larger libraries needed.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude study.

evidence.quote_or_span: the 1384 FDA drug dataset was too small to find novel nanomolar inhibitors, thus further screening of various novel compound sets will be undertaken

validation_methods: Conservation and mutation-activity analysis of anchor residues
Binding analysis of 89 known NS3 protease inhibitors
In vitro MTT cytotoxicity and DENV plaque formation assays
success_verification.final_conclusion: Pharmacophore anchor models successfully identified repurposable FDA drugs (telaprevir, asunaprevir) with anti-DENV activity, with SAAR studies explaining activity differences by anchor occupancy, particularly CEH1.
limitations: FDA drug dataset too small (1384 compounds) to find nanomolar inhibitors
Docking inaccuracies for some inhibitors led to incorrect anchor occupancy predictions

source.title: Machine learning-assisted directed protein evolution with combinatorial libraries

initial_request.research_problem: Directed protein evolution is limited by the high experimental cost of screening large combinatorial libraries. The paper proposes integrating machine learning to predict protein fitness in silico, reducing the number of variants that need to be tested experimentally.
initial_request.target_object: Protein fitness landscape (GB1 binding protein) and enzyme enantioselectivity (Rma NOD)
initial_request.known_context: Combinatorial sequence space is expensive to sample experimentally. Machine learning models trained on tested variants can provide a fast method for testing sequence space computationally.
constraints: Equal screening burden compared to traditional directed evolution methods
Use of shallow neural networks
Training on random samples of combinatorial libraries
input_data: GB1 fitness landscape (149,361 out of 160,000 variants)
quantifiable_goals: Reach global optimum fitness more often than single-mutant walk or recombination
Reduce number of variants needed for similar results
Achieve high enantioselectivity in enzyme variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Combinatorial sequence space is expensive to sample experimentally.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to reduce experimental effort while exploring combinatorial sequence space effectively.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Machine learning models trained on tested variants can computationally test sequence space, reducing experimental effort.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use machine learning to predict protein fitness in silico and guide library design.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Machine learning models trained on tested variants provide a fast method for testing sequence space computationally.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design an approach to train models on random samples and predict enriched libraries.

evidence.quote_or_span: Combinatorial sequence space can be quite expensive to sample experimentally
machine learning models trained on tested variants provide a fast method for testing sequence space computationally

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that ML can reduce experimental effort.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to implement ML-assisted evolution with random sampling and library prediction.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Training on a random sample of a combinatorial library and predicting a restricted library enriched in high fitness will reduce experimental burden.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use random sample of combinatorial library to train models, then predict a smaller set of variants encoded with degenerate codons.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Data from a random sample of the combinatorial library are used to train machine learning models, which predict a smaller set of variants encoded with degenerate codons.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Implement the design with specific numbers: train shallow neural networks on 470 variants and predict 100.

evidence.quote_or_span: data from a random sample of the combinatorial library, the input library, are used to train machine learning models. These models are used to predict a smaller set of variants, the predicted library, which can be encoded with degenerate codons to test experimentally
shallow neural networks were trained with 470 randomly-selected input variants, from which 100 predictions were tested

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Design requires specific implementation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to set up computational experiment with concrete numbers.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Shallow neural networks trained with 470 randomly-selected input variants can predict 100 variants for testing.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use shallow neural networks, 470 training variants, 100 predictions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Shallow neural networks were trained with 470 randomly-selected input variants, from which 100 predictions were tested.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the approach in simulated directed evolution on GB1 fitness landscape.

evidence.quote_or_span: shallow neural networks were trained with 470 randomly-selected input variants, from which 100 predictions were tested

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Computational setup ready.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does ML-assisted evolution outperform traditional methods under equal screening burden?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ML-assisted evolution will reach higher fitness than single-mutant walk or recombination when testing the same number of variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Simulate directed evolution on GB1 landscape comparing ML, single-mutant walk, and recombination with 570 variants each.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Simulations compared final fitnesses reached by ML-assisted evolution, single-mutant walk, and recombination with equal screening burden.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze results: frequency of reaching global optimum.

evidence.quote_or_span: we compare the final fitnesses reached by simulated directed evolution with and without machine learning, based on testing the same number of variants

Assuming each amino acid occurs with equal frequency and that the library has complete coverage, applying the 3-fold oversampling rule to obtain roughly 95% library coverage (18, 19) results in a total of 570 variants screened (SI Appendix, Library Coverage).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Simulation results obtained.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Quantify advantage of ML over traditional methods in reaching global optimum.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ML-assisted evolution reaches global optimum more often.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate percentage of simulations reaching global optimum for each method.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ML-assisted evolution reaches global optimum in 8.2% of simulations vs. 4.9% for single-mutant walk and 4.0% for recombination.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Further analyze efficiency: how many fewer variants does ML need?

evidence.quote_or_span: machine learning-assisted evolution reaches the global optimum fitness value in 8.2% of 600 simulations, compared to 4.9% of all starting sequences reaching the same value through a single-mutant walk and 4.0% of simulated recombination runs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ML reaches global optimum more often.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How many fewer variants does ML require for similar results?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ML approach requires fewer variants to achieve results similar to single-mutant walk.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare number of variants needed for similar final fitness.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The machine-learning approach requires about 30% fewer variants to achieve final results similar to the single-mutant walk.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Compare expected fitness values.

evidence.quote_or_span: the machine-learning approach requires about 30% fewer variants to achieve final results similar to the single-mutant walk with this analysis.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ML requires fewer variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the expected fitness of each method?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ML approach has higher expected fitness.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate expected fitness values for each method.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ML approach expected fitness 6.42 vs. single-mutant walk 5.41 and recombination 5.93.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess model accuracy needed for effective guidance.

evidence.quote_or_span: the machine learning approach has an expected fitness value of 6.42, compared to 5.41 and 5.93 for the single step walk and recombination, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Method validated on GB1 simulations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can ML-assisted evolution improve enzyme enantioselectivity experimentally?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Training on random sequence-function pairs can predict libraries with improved enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Round 1: Train models on 124 sequence-function pairs, predict libraries, screen 90 variants each for (S)- and (R)-enantiomers.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified VCHV (93% ee (S)) and GSSG (62% ee (R)).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use best variants as parents for Round 2.

evidence.quote_or_span: From 124 sequence–function relationships sampled randomly, models were trained to predict a restricted set of selective variants.

From the predicted libraries for both enantiomers, two variants, called VCHV (93 % ee) and GSSG (62 % ee) for their amino acids at positions 32, 46, 56, and 97, were identified by screening 90 variants for each.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Round 1 identified VCHV and GSSG.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can further improvement be achieved by recombining beneficial mutations?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Using VCHV and GSSG as parents, predicted libraries will yield variants with higher enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Round 2: Train models on new data, predict libraries, screen variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Obtained variants with 93% ee (S) and 79% ee (R).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Summarize total experimental effort.

evidence.quote_or_span: From those predicted libraries, we obtained two variants with measured enantioselectivities of 93% and 79% ee for the (S)- and (R)-enantiomers, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: VCHV was most selective in initial screen.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: VCHV's selectivity decreased in final validation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Initial screen may overestimate selectivity due to screening conditions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation: VCHV less selective in final validation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: VCHV was the most selective variant in the initial screen, but was less selective in final validation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Highlight diverse solutions found, e.g., position 49 tolerance.

evidence.quote_or_span: VCHV was the most selective variant in the initial screen, but was less selective in final validation.
However, the 49P 51V 53I variant from VCHV has higher total activity under screening conditions.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Evolution identified multiple solutions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does ML-assisted evolution find diverse solutions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ML-assisted evolution can identify diverse solutions in protein space.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze amino acid tolerance at position 49.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Proline conserved in two most (S)-selective variants; tyrosine and arginine tolerated with <1% loss in enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitation: predictive accuracy could be improved.

evidence.quote_or_span: proline is conserved at residue 49 in two of the most (S)-selective variants.
tyrosine and arginine are also tolerated at position 49 with less than 1% loss in enantioselectivity.
This suggests that there are diverse solutions in protein space for specific properties

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Method successful but has limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Predictive accuracy could be improved.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: More data or computationally expensive simulations could improve accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation and suggest future improvements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Predictive accuracy could be improved by more data or simulations, but improved variants can already be identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude trajectory.

evidence.quote_or_span: While predictive accuracy could be improved by more computationally-expensive simulations or by collecting more data for machine learning, improved variants can already be identified by sampling from a space predicted to be dense in higher fitness variants.

validation_methods: Simulated directed evolution on GB1 fitness landscape
Experimental validation with enzyme enantioselectivity assays
metrics: Percentage of simulations reaching global optimum
success_verification.final_conclusion: Machine learning-assisted directed evolution can efficiently explore combinatorial sequence space, reaching higher fitness with fewer variants than traditional methods, and successfully engineered enantioselective enzymes.
limitations: VCHV selectivity decreased in final validation
Predictive accuracy could be improved with more data or simulations
